source: If you find yourself resetting the clock in your sleep, it can be placed on the other side of the room, forcing you to get out of bed to turn it off.
reference: Jekk issib lilek innifsek tirrissettja l-arloġġ waqt l-irqad tiegħek, tista’ tpoġġih fuq in-naħa l-oħra tal-kamra, u dan jġiegħlek toħroġ mis-sodda biex titfih.

source: Other biorhythm-based options involve drinking lots of fluid (particularly water or tea, a known diuretic) prior to sleep, forcing one to get up to urinate.
reference: Alternattivi oħrajn fuq il-bażi tal-bijoritmi jinvolvu x-xorb ta' ħafna fluwidi (b'mod partikolari ilma jew te, dijuretiku popolari) qabel l-irqad, biex b’hekk iġiegħlu lill-persuna tqum biex tgħaddi l-awrina.

source: The amount of inner peace a person possesses correlates oppositely to the amount of tension in one’s body and spirit.
reference: L-ammont ta’ paċi interna li jkollha persuna hija korrelatata b’mod oppost mal-ammont ta’ tensjoni fil-ġisem u l-ispirtu tal-persuna.

source: The lower the tension, the more positive the life force present. Every person has the potential to find absolute peace and contentment.
reference: Iktar ma tkun baxxa t-tensjoni, iktar tkun pożittiva l-forza tal-ħajja preżenti. Kull persuna għandha l-potenzjal li ssib paċi u kuntentizza assoluta.

source: Everyone can achieve enlightenment. The only thing standing in the way of this goal is our own tension and negativity.
reference: Kulħadd jista’ jsir illuminat. L-unika ostaklu għal dan huwa t-tensjoni u n-negattività tagħna stess.

source: The Tibetan Buddhism is based on the teachings of Buddha, but were extended by the mahayana path of love and by a lot of techniques from Indian Yoga.
reference: Il-Buddiżmu Tibetan huwa bbażat fuq it-tagħlimiet ta' Buddha, imma kienu mwessgħin bil-mogħdija tal-imħabba ta' mahayana u ħafna tekniki mill-Joga Indjana.

source: In principle the Tibetan Buddhism is very simple. It consists of Kundalini Yoga, meditation and the path of all-embracing love.
reference: Bħala prinċipju, il-Buddiżmu Tibetan huwa sempliċi ħafna. Huwa jikkonsisti f’Yoga Kundalini, meditazzjoni u l-idea li l-imħabba taċċetta kollox.

source: With Kundalini Yoga the Kundalini energy (enlightenment energy) is awakened through yoga postures, breathing exercises, mantras and visualizations.
reference: Bil-Joga ta' Kundalini l-enerġija ta' Kundalini (enerġija mdawla) titqajjem permezz tal-pożi tal-joga, l-eżerċizzi tan-nifs, il-mantras u l-viżwalizzazzjonijiet.

source: The center of Tibetan meditation is the Deity Yoga. Through the visualization of various deities the energy channels are cleaned, the chakras are activated and the enlightenment consciousness is created.
reference: Fil-qalba tal-meditazzjoni Tibetana hemm il-Yoga tal-Allat. Permezz tal-viżwalizzazzjoni ta’ diversi allat wieħed inaddaf il-kanali tal-enerġija, jattiva x-chakras u joħloq kuxjenza ta’ illuminazzjoni.

source: Germany was a common enemy in World War 2, leading to cooperation between the USSR and USA. With the end of the war the clashes of system, process and culture led to the countries falling out.
reference: Il-Ġermanja kienet għadu komuni fit-Tieni Gwerra Dinjija, u wasslet għal kooperazzjoni bejn il-USSR u l-USA. Mat-tmiem tal-gwerra in-nuqqas ta’ qbil fis-sistema, il-proċess u l-kultura wassal biex il-pajjiżi jiġġieldu.

source: With two years of the end of the war, the former allies were now enemies and the Cold War began.
reference: Wara biss sentejn minn tmiem il-gwerra, dawk li qabel kienu alleati issa saru għedewwa u bdiet il-Gwerra Bierda.

source: It was to last for the next 40 years and would be fought for real, by proxy armies, on battlefields from Africa to Asia, in Afghanistan, Cuba and many other places.
reference: Kellha ddum għall-40 sena li ġejjin u kellha tiġi miġġielda ta’ veru, minn armati alternattivi, fuq kampijiet tal-battalja mill-Afrika sal-Asja, fl-Afganistan, Kuba u ħafna postijiet oħra.

source: By September 17, 1939, the Polish defense was already broken, and the only hope was to retreat and reorganise along the Romanian bridgehead.
reference: Sas-17 ta’ Settembru 1939, id-difiża Pollakka kienet diġà maqsuma, u l-unika tama kienet li tirtira u terġa’ torganizza ruħha mill-ġdid tul il-pont Rumen.

source: However, these plans were rendered obsolete nearly overnight, when over 800,000 soldiers from the Soviet's Union Red Army entered and created the Belarussian and Ukrainian fronts after invading the eastern regions of Poland in violation of the Riga Peace Treaty, the Soviet-Polish Non-Aggression Pact, and other international treaties, both bilateral and multilateral.
reference: Madankollu, dawn il-pjanijiet saru obsoleti kważi mil-lum għal għada, meta daħlu aktar minn 800,000 suldat mill-Armata Ħamra tal-Unjoni Sovjetika u ħolqu l-fronti tal-Belarussja u tal-Ukraina wara li invadew ir-reġjuni tal-Lvant tal-Polonja bi ksur tat-Trattat tal-Paċi ta’ Riga, il-Patt ta’ Non-Aggressjoni Sovjetiku-Pollakk, u trattati internazzjonali oħra, kemm bilaterali kif ukoll multilaterali.

source: Using ships to transport goods is by far the most efficient way to move large amounts of people and goods across oceans.
reference: L-użu tal-vapuri għat-trasport tal-merkanzija huwa bla dubju l-aktar mod effiċjenti biex iċċaqlaq ammonti kbar ta' nies u oġġetti biex jaqsmu l-oċeani.

source: The job of navies has traditionally been to ensure that your country maintains the ability to move your people and goods, while at the same time, interfering with your enemy's ability to move his people and goods.
reference: Ix-xogħol tan-navies tradizzjonalment kien li jiżguraw li pajjiżek iżomm l-abbiltà li jċaqlaq in-nies u l-oġġetti tiegħek, filwaqt li fl-istess ħin, jinterferixxu mal-kapaċità tal-għadu tiegħek li jċaqlaq in-nies u l-oġġetti tiegħu.

source: One of the most noteworthy recent examples of this was the North Atlantic campaign of WWII. The Americans were trying to move men and materials across the Atlantic Ocean to help Britain.
reference: Wieħed mill-eżempji reċenti l-iktar notevoli ta’ dan kienet il-kampanja tal-Atlantiku tat-Tramuntana tat-Tieni Gwerra Dinjija. L-Amerikani kienu qed jippruvaw iċaqilqu l-irġiel u l-materjali n-naħa l-oħra tal-Oċean Atlantiku biex jgħinu lill-Gran Brittanja.

source: At the same time, the German navy, using mainly U-boats, was trying to stop this traffic.
reference: Fl-istess ħin, il-flotta Ġermaniża, li prinċipalment tuża U-boats, kienet qed tipprova twaqqaf dan it-traffiku.

source: Had the Allies failed, Germany probably would have been able to conquer Britain as it had the rest of Europe.
reference: Kieku fallew l-Alleati, il-Ġermanja probabbilment kienet tkun kapaċi tegħleb lill-Gran Brittanja bħal ma għamlet lill-bqija tal-Ewropa.

source: Goats seem to have been first domesticated roughly 10,000 years ago in the Zagros Mountains of Iran.
reference: Jirriżulta li l-mogħoż ġew domestikati l-ewwel madwar 10,000 sena ilu fil-Muntanji Zagros tal-Iran.

source: Ancient cultures and tribes began to keep them for easy access to milk, hair, meat, and skins.
reference: Il-kulturi u t-tribujiet tal-antik bdew iżommuhom biex ikollhom mod faċli kif jakkwistaw ħalib, suf, laħam, u ġlud.

source: Domestic goats were generally kept in herds that wandered on hills or other grazing areas, often tended by goatherds who were frequently children or adolescents, similar to the more widely known shepherd. These methods of herding are still used today.
reference: Il-mogħoż domestiċi ġeneralment kienu miżmuma f’merħliet li jimirħu mal-għoljiet jew żoni oħra ta’ mergħa, u ħafna drabi kienu jattenduhom ir-rgħajja tal-mogħoż li spiss kienu tfal jew adolexxenti, simili għar-rgħajja tan-ngħaġ iktar magħrufin. Dawn il-metodi ta’ għassa tal-merħliet għadhom jintużaw sal-lum.

source: Wagonways were built in England as early as the 16th Century.
reference: Wagonways inbnew fl-Ingilterra kmieni fis-Seklu 16.

source: Although wagonways merely consisted of parallel planks of wood, they allowed horses pulling them to achieve greater speeds and pull larger loads than on the slightly more rough roads of the day.
reference: Għalkemm il-wagonways kienu jikkonsistu sempliċement fi planki paralleli tal-injam, huma kienu jippermettu liż-żwiemel li jiġbduhom biex jiksbu veloċitajiet akbar u jiġbdu tagħbijiet akbar milli fuq it-toroq ftit aktar raffi ta' dak iż-żmien.

source: Crossties were introduced fairly early to hold the tracks in place. Gradually, however, it was realised that tracks would be more efficient if they had a stip of iron on the top.
reference: Il-”crossties” (għoqiedi msallbin) ġew introdotti pjuttost kmieni biex iżommu l-binarji f’posthom. Madankollu, b'mod gradwali, saret ir-realizzazzjoni li l-binarji jkunu iktar effiċjenti jekk ikollhom strixxa tal-ħadid fil-parti ta’ fuq.

source: This became common practice, but the iron caused more wear on the wooden wheels of the wagons.
reference: Din saret prattika komuni, imma l-ħadid ikkaġuna aktar kedd fuq ir-roti tal-injam tal-karettuni.

source: Eventually, wooden wheels were replaced by iron wheels. In 1767, the first full-iron rails were introduced.
reference: Eventwalment, ir-roti tal-injam ġew mibdula b’roti tal-ħadid. Fl-1767, ġew introdotti l-ewwel linji kompletament tal-ħadid.

source: The first known transportation was walking, humans began walking upright two million years ago with the emergence of Homo Erectus (meaning upright man).
reference: L-ewwel mezz tat-trasport magħruf kien il-mixi, il-bnedmin bdew jimxu bil-wieqfa żewġ miljuni ta’ snin ilu meta tfaċċa Homo Erectus (li tfisser bniedem bil-wieqfa).

source: Their predecessors, the Australopithecus did not walk upright as habitually.
reference: Il-predeċessuri tagħhom, l-Australopithecus ma kinux jimxu  bil-wieqfa b'mod abitwali.

source: Bipedal specializations are found in Australopithecus fossils from 4.2-3.9 million years ago, although Sahelanthropus may have walked on two legs as early as seven million years ago.
reference: Speċjalizzazzjonijiet bipedali jinstabu fil-fossili tal-Australopithecus minn 4.2 sa 3.9 miljun sena ilu, għalkemm is-Sahelanthropus seta’ mexa fuq żewġ saqajn saħansitra minn seba’ miljun sena ilu.

source: We can start living more friendly to the environment, we can join to the environmental movement, and we can even be activists in order to reduce the future suffering in some degree.
reference: Nistgħu nibdew ngħixu aktar favur l-ambjent, nistgħu ningħaqdu mal-moviment ambjentali, u nistgħu anki nkunu attivisti sabiex innaqqsu t-tbatija futuri b'xi grad.

source: This is just like symptomatic treatment in many cases. However, if we do not only want a temporary solution, then we should find the root of the problems, and we should deactivate them.
reference: Dan huwa simili għal trattament sintomatiku f'ħafna każijiet. Minkejja dan, jekk ma rridux biss soluzzjoni temporanja, għandna nsibu s-sors tal-problemi, u għandna niddiżattivawh.

source: It is obvious enough that the world has changed much because of humankind's scientific and technological advancements, and problems have become greater because of overpopulation and mankind's extravagant lifestyle.
reference: Huwa ovvju biżżejjed li d-dinja nbidlet ħafna minħabba l-avvanzi xjentifiċi u teknoloġiċi tal-umanità, u l-problemi saru akbar minħabba l-popolazzjoni żejda u l-istil ta’ ħajja stravaganti tal-umanità.

source: After its adoption by Congress on July 4, a handwritten draft signed by the President of Congress John Hancock and the Secretary Charles Thomson was then sent a few blocks away to the printing shop of John Dunlap.
reference: Wara l-adozzjoni tiegħu mill-Kungress fl-4 ta' Lulju, l-abbozz miktub bl-idejn u ffirmat mill-President tal-Kungress John Hancock u s-Segretarju Charles Thomson imbagħad intbagħat ftit blokki 'l bogħod fil-ħanut tal-istamperija ta' John Dunlap.

source: Through the night between 150 and 200 copies were made, now known as "Dunlap broadsides".
reference: Matul il-lejl saru bejn 150 u 200 kopja, issa magħrufin bħala "id-Dunlap broadsides".

source: The first public reading of the document was by John Nixon in the yard of Independence Hall on July 8.
reference: L-ewwel qari pubbliku tad-dokument sar minn John Nixon fil-bitħa tal-Independence Hall fit-8 ta’ Lulju.

source: One was sent to George Washington on July 6, who had it read to his troops in New York on July 9. A copy reached London on August 10.
reference: Waħda ntbagħtet lil George Washington fis-6 ta’ Lulju, li ordna li din tinqara lit-truppi tiegħu ġewwa New York fid-9 ta’ Lulju. Kopja waslet Londra fl-10 ta’ Awwissu.

source: The 25 Dunlap broadsides still known to exist are the oldest surviving copies of the document. The original handwritten copy has not survived.
reference: Il-25 broadsides Dunlap li għadhom magħrufa li jeżistu huma l-eqdem kopji li baqgħu fis-seħħ tad-dokument. Il-kopja oriġinali miktuba bl-idejn ma għadhiex teżisti.

source: Many paleontologists today believe that one group of dinosaurs survived and is alive today. We call them birds.
reference: Ħafna paleontoloġisti llum jemmnu li grupp wieħed ta’ dinosawri baqa’ ħaj u għadu jeżisti llum il-ġurnata. Aħna nsejħulhom għasafar.

source: Many people don't think about them as dinosaurs because they have feathers and can fly.
reference: Ħafna nies ma jarawhomx bħala dinosawri għax għandhom ir-rix u jistgħu jtiru.

source: But there are a lot of things about birds that still look like a dinosaur.
reference: Iżda hemm ħafna affarijiet dwar l-għasafar li għadhom jidhru qishom dinosawri.

source: They have feet with scales and claws, they lay eggs, and they walk on their two back legs like a T-Rex.
reference: Huma għandhom saqajn bil-qxur u dwiefer bil-ponta, huma jbidu l-bajd, u jimxu fuq saqajhom ta' wara bħal T-Rex.

source: Virtually all computers in use today are based on the manipulation of information which is coded in the form of binary numbers.
reference: Prattikament il-kompjuters kollha li jintużaw illum huma bbażati fuq il-manipulazzjoni tal-informazzjoni li hija kkodifikata f'forma ta' numri binarji.

source: A binary number can have only one of two values, i.e. 0 or 1, and these numbers are referred to as binary digits - or bits, to use computer jargon.
reference: Numru binarju jista' jkollu biss valur wieħed minn tnejn, jiġifieri 0 jew 1, u dawn in-numri jissejħu ċifri binarji - jew bits, fil-lingwaġġ tal-kompjuter.

source: Internal poisoning may not be immediately apparent. Symptoms, such as vomiting are sufficiently general that an immediate diagnosis cannot be made.
reference: L-avvelenament intern jista’ ma jidhirx immedjatament. Sintomi bħar-rimettar huma ġenerali biżżejjed li ma tkunx tista’ ssir dijanjosi immedjata.

source: The best indication of internal poisoning may be the presence of an open container of medication or toxic household chemicals.
reference: L-aħjar indikazzjoni ta’ avvelenament intern tista’ tkun il-preżenza ta’ kontenitur miftuħ ta’ medikazzjoni jew kimiċi tossiċi tad-dar.

source: Check the label for specific first aid instructions for that specific poison.
reference: Iċċekkja t-tikketta għal struzzjonijiet speċifiċi tal-ewwel għajnuna għal dak il-velenu speċifiku.

source: The term bug is used by entomologists in a formal sense for this group of insects.
reference: It-terminu bug huwa użat mill-entomoloġisti f'sens formali għal dan il-grupp ta' insetti.

source: This term derives from ancient familiarity with Bed-bugs, which are insects highly adapted to parasitize humans.
reference: Dan it-terminu ġej mill-familjarità antika mal-Baqq, li huma insetti adatti ħafna bħala parassiti tal-bnedmin.

source: Both Assassin-bugs and Bed-bugs are nidicolous, adapted to living in nest or housing of their host.
reference: Kemm l-Assassin-bugs kif ukoll il-Bed-bugs huma nidikoli, adattati biex jgħixu f’bejta jew fil-post tal-ospitant tagħhom.

source: Across the United States of America, there are approximately 400,000 known cases of Multiple Sclerosis (MS), leaving it as the leading neurological disease in younger and middle aged adults.
reference: Madwar l-Istati Uniti tal-Amerka, hemm madwar 400,000 każ magħruf ta' Sklerosi Multipla (MS), li jagħmilha l-marda newroloġika ewlenija fl-adulti żgħar u ta' età medja.

source: MS is a disease that affects the central nervous system, which is made up of the brain, the spinal cord and the optic nerve.
reference: MS hija marda li taffettwa s-sistema nervuża ċentrali, li hija magħmula mill-moħħ, is-spina dorsali u n-nerv ottiku.

source: Research has found that females are two times more likely to have MS then males.
reference: Ir-riċerka sabet li n-nisa huma darbtejn aktar probabbli li jkollhom MS milli l-irġiel.

source: A couple may decide it is not in their best interest, or in the interest of their child, to raise a baby.
reference: Koppja tista' tiddeċiedi li mhuwiex fl-aħjar interess tagħha, jew fl-interess tal-wild tagħha, li trabbi tarbija.

source: These couples may choose to make an adoption plan for their baby.
reference: Dawn il-koppji jistgħu jagħżlu li jagħmlu pjan ta' adozzjoni għat-tarbija tagħhom.

source: In an adoption, the birth parents terminate their parental rights so that another couple may parent the child.
reference: F’adozzjoni, il-ġenituri bijoloġiċi itemmu d-drittijiet tal-ġenituri tagħhom sabiex koppja oħra jkunu jistgħu jsiru l-ġenituri tat-tifel/tifla.

source: Science’s main goal is to figure out the way the world works through the scientific method. This method in fact guides most scientific research.
reference: L-għan ewlieni tax-xjenza huwa li tifhem il-mod kif taħdem id-dinja permezz tal-metodu xjentifiku. Dan il-metodu fil-fatt jiggwida ħafna mir-riċerka xjentifika.

source: It isn’t alone though, experimentation, and an experiment is a test that is used to eliminate one or more of the possible hypotheses, asking questions, and making observations also guide scientific research.
reference: Madankollu mhijiex waħedha, l-esperimentazzjoni, u esperiment huwa test li jintuża biex tiġi eliminata waħda jew aktar mill-ipoteżijiet possibbli, li jsiru mistoqsijiet, u li jsiru osservazzjonijiet huwa ta’ gwida wkoll għar-riċerka xjentifika.

source: Naturalists and philosophers focused on classical texts and, in particular, on the Bible in Latin.
reference: In-naturalisti u l-filosofi ffukaw fuq testi klassiċi u, b’mod partikolari, fuq il-Bibbja bil-Latin.

source: Accepted were Aristotle's views on all matters of science, including psychology.
reference: Kienu aċċettati l-fehmiet ta' Aristotli fuq is-suġġetti kollha tax-xjenza, inkluża l-psikoloġija.

source: As knowledge of Greek declined, the West found itself cut off from its Greek philosophical and scientific roots.
reference: Meta naqas l-għarfien tal-lingwa Griega, il-Punent sab ruħu maqtugħ mill-għeruq Griegi filosofiċi u xjentifiċi tiegħu.

source: Many observed rhythms in physiology and behavior often crucially depend on the presence of endogenous cycles and their production through biological clocks.
reference: Ħafna ritmi osservati fil-fiżjoloġija u l-imġieba spiss jiddependu b’mod kruċjali fuq il-preżenza ta’ ċikli endoġeni u l-produzzjoni tagħhom permezz ta’ arloġġi bijoloġiċi.

source: Periodic rhythms, which are not simply responses to external periodic cues, have been documented for most living beings, including bacteria, fungi, plants, and animals.
reference: Ritmi perjodiċi, li mhumiex sempliċement risposti għal indikazzjonijiet perjodiċi esterni, ġew dokumentati għall-biċċa l-kbira tal-kreaturi ħajjin, inklużi batterji, fungi, pjanti u annimali.

source: Biological clocks are self sustaining oscillators which will continue a period of free-running cycling even in the absence of external cues.
reference: L-arloġġi bijoloġiċi huma oxxillaturi li jsostnu lilhom infushom li se jkomplu perjodu ta’ ċikliżmu ta’ ġiri liberu anke mingħajr indikazzjonijiet esterni.

source: The Hershey and Chase experiment was one of the leading suggestions that DNA was a genetic material.
reference: L-esperiment ta’ Hershey u Chase kien wieħed mis-suġġerimenti ewlenin li d-DNA kien materjal ġenetiku.

source: Hershey and Chase used phages, or viruses, to implant their own DNA into a bacterium.
reference: Hershey u Chase użaw fagi, jew vajrusis, biex idaħħlu d-DNA tagħhom stess ġewwa bacterium.

source: They did two experiments marking either the DNA in the phage with a radioactive phosphorus or the protein of the phage with radioactive sulfur.
reference: Huma għamlu żewġ esperimenti billi mmarkaw jew id-DNA fil-batterjofagu b'fosfru radjuattiv jew il-proteina tal-batterjofagu b'kubrit radjuattiv.

source: Mutations can have a variety of different effects depending on the type of mutation, the significance of the piece of genetic material affected and whether the cells affected are germ-line cells.
reference: Il-mutazzjonijiet jista’ jkollhom varjetà ta’ effetti differenti skont it-tip ta’ mutazzjoni, is-sinifikat tal-biċċa ta’ materjal ġenetiku affettwat u jekk iċ-ċelloli affettwati humiex ċelloli tal-linja tal-mikrobi.

source: Only mutations in germ-line cells can be passed on to children, while mutations elsewhere can cause cell-death or cancer.
reference: Mutazzjonijiet biss fiċ-ċelloli tal-linja tal-mikrobi jistgħu jiġu mgħoddija lit-tfal, filwaqt li mutazzjonijiet x’imkien ieħor jistgħu jikkawżaw mewt taċ-ċelloli jew kanċer.

source: Nature-based tourism attracts people interested in visiting natural areas for the purpose of enjoying the scenery, including plant and animal wildlife.
reference: It-turiżmu bbażat fuq in-natura jattira nies interessati li jżuru żoni aturali sabiex igawdu x-xenarju, inklużi l-pjanti u l-annimali selvaġġi.

source: Examples of on-site activities include hunting, fishing, photography, bird watching, and visiting parks and studying information about the ecosystem.
reference: Eżempji ta’ attivitajiet fuq il-post jinkludu l-kaċċa, is-sajd, il-fotografija, l-osservazzjoni tal-għasafar, u żjarat fil-parks u l-istudju ta’ informazzjoni dwar l-ekosistema.

source: An example is visiting, photographing, and learning about organgatuangs in Borneo.
reference: Eżempju ta' dan huwa li żżur, tieħu r-ritratti, u titgħallem dwar l-orangutani f'Borneo.

source: Every morning, people leave small country towns in cars to go their workplace and are passed by others whose work destination is the place they have just left.
reference: Kull filgħodu, in-nies jitilqu mill-bliet iż-żgħar tal-pajjiż bil-karozzi biex imorru x-xogħol u jgħaddu minn ħdejhom persuni oħrajn li l-post tax-xogħol tagħhom huwa l-post li għadhom kemm telqu minnu.

source: In this dynamic transport shuttle everyone is somehow connected with, and supporting, a transport system based on private cars.
reference: F’dan ix-shuttle dinamiku tat-trasport kulħadd, b’xi mod, huwa marbut ma', u jappoġġja, sistema ta’ trasport ibbażata fuq karozzi privati.

source: Science now indicates that this massive carbon economy has dislodged the biosphere from one of its stable states that has supported human evolution for the past two million years.
reference: Ix-xjenza issa tindika li din l-ekonomija massiva tal-karbonju ċaqilqet il-bijosfera minn wieħed mill-istati stabbli tagħha li appoġġja l-evoluzzjoni tal-bniedem għal dawn l-aħħar żewġ miljuni ta’ snin.

source: Everyone participates in society and uses transportation systems. Almost everyone complains about transportation systems.
reference: Kulħadd jieħu sehem fis-soċjetà u juża s-sistemi tat-trasport. Kważi kulħadd igerger dwar is-sistemi tat-trasport.

source: In developed countries you seldom hear similar levels of complaints about water quality or bridges falling down.
reference: Fil-pajjiżi żviluppati, rari tisma’ livelli ta' tgergir simili dwar il-kwalità tal-ilma jew pontijiet li jaqgħu.

source: Why do transportation systems engender such complaints, why do they fail on a daily basis? Are transportation engineers just incompetent? Or is something more fundamental going on?
reference: Għaliex is-sistemi tat-trasport joħolqu ilmenti bħal dawn, għaliex ifallu ta' kuljum? L-inġiniera tat-trasport huma sempliċiment inkompetenti? Jew qed jiġri xi ħaġa aktar fundamentali?

source: Traffic Flow is the study of the movement of individual drivers and vehicles between two points and the interactions they make with one another.
reference: Il-Fluss tat-Traffiku huwa l-istudju tal-moviment ta' sewwieqa u vetturi individwali bejn żewġ postijiet u l-interazzjonijiet li jagħmlu ma' xulxin.

source: Unfortunately, studying traffic flow is difficult because driver behavior cannot be predicted with one-hundred percent certainty.
reference: Sfortunatament, l-istudju tal-fluss tat-traffiku huwa diffiċli għax l-imġiba tas-sewwieqa ma tistax tiġi mbassra b'ċertezza ta' mija fil-mija.

source: Fortunately, drivers tend to behave within a reasonably consistent range; thus, traffic streams tend to have some reasonable consistency and can be roughly represented mathematically.
reference: Fortunatament, is-sewwieqa għandhom tendenza li jġibu ruħhom f’medda raġonevolment konsistenti; għalhekk, il-flussi tat-traffiku għandhom tendenza li jkollhom konsistenza raġonevoli u jistgħu jiġu rappreżentati bejn wieħed u ieħor matematikament.

source: To better represent traffic flow, relationships have been established between the three main characteristics: (1) flow, (2) density, and (3) velocity.
reference: Biex il-fluss tat-traffiku jiġi rrappreżentat aħjar, ġew stabbiliti relazzjonijiet bejn it-tliet karatteristiċi ewlenin: (1) il-fluss, (2) id-densità, u (3) l-veloċità.

source: These relationships help in planning, design, and operations of roadway facilities.
reference: Dawn ir-relazzjonijiet jgħinu fl-ippjanar, id-disinn, u t-tħaddim tal-faċilitajiet tat-triq.

source: Insects were the first animals to take to the air. Their ability to fly helped them evade enemies more easily and find food and mates more efficiently.
reference: L-insetti kienu l-ewwel annimali li bdew itiru. Il-kapaċità tagħhom biex itiru għenithom jevitaw l-għedewwa b'mod iktar faċli u jsibu l-ikel u s-sieħba b’mod iktar effiċjenti.

source: Most insects have the advantage of being able to fold their wings back along the body.
reference: Il-biċċa l-kbira tal-insetti għandhom il-vantaġġ li jkunu jistgħu jitwu l-ġwienaħ tagħhom lura f’posthom tul ġisimhom.

source: This gives them a wider range of small places to hide from predators.
reference: Dan jagħtihom firxa aktar wiesgħa ta' postijiet żgħar biex jistaħbew mill-predaturi.

source: Today, the only insects that cannot fold back their wings are dragon flies and mayflies.
reference: Illum, l-uniċi insetti li ma jistgħux jitwu lura l-ġwienaħ tagħhom huma l-mazzarell u l-mayflies.

source: Thousands of years ago, a man called Aristarchus said that the Solar System moved around the Sun.
reference: Eluf ta' snin ilu, bniedem imsejjaħ Aristarchus qal li s-Sistema Solari tiċċaqlaq madwar ix-Xemx.

source: Some people thought he was right but many people believed the opposite; that the Solar System moved around the Earth, including the Sun (and even the other stars).
reference: Xi nies ħasbu li kellu raġun imma ħafna nies emmnu l-oppost; li s-Sistema Solari mxiet madwar id-Dinja, inkluż ix-Xemx (u anke l-istilel l-oħra).

source: This seems sensible, because the Earth doesn't feel as if it's moving, does it?
reference: Dan jidher sensibbli, għax id-Dinja ma tinħassx li qed tiċċaqlaq, mhux hekk?

source: The Amazon River is the second longest and the biggest river on Earth. It carries more than 8 times as much water as the second biggest river.
reference: Ix-Xmara tal-Amażonja hija t-tieni l-itwal u l-ikbar xmara fid-Dinja. Hija ġġorr iktar minn 8 darbiet l-ilma tat-tieni l-ikbar xmara.

source: The Amazon is also the widest river on Earth, at times six miles wide.
reference: L-Amażonja hija wkoll l-iktar xmara wiesgħa fid-Dinja, f’xi punti għandha wisa' ta' sitt mili.

source: A full 20 percent of the water that pours out of the planet's rivers into the oceans comes from the Amazon.
reference: B'kollox, mill-Amażonja jiġu 20 fil-mija tal-ilma kollu li jiġi mferra mix-xmajjar tal-pjaneta ġol-oċeani.

source: The main Amazon River is 6,387 km (3,980 miles). It collects water from thousands of smaller rivers.
reference: Ix-Xmara tal-Amażonja ewlenija hija 6,387 km (3,980 mil). Din tiġbor ilma minn eluf ta’ xmajjar iżgħar.

source: Although pyramid-building in stone continued until the end of the Old Kingdom, the pyramids of Giza were never surpassed in their size and the technical excellence of their construction.
reference: Għalkemm il-bini tal-piramidi bil-ġebel kompla sal-aħħar tas-Saltna l-Antika, il-piramidi ta' Giża baqgħu l-ikbar fid-daqs u l-aqwa fl-eċċellenza teknika tal-kostruzzjoni tagħhom.

source: New Kingdom ancient Egyptians marvelled at their predecessors monuments, which were then well over a thousand year old.
reference: L-Eġizzjani tal-antik tar-Renju l-Ġdid kienu mmeraviljati bil-monumenti tal-predeċessuri tagħhom li, dak iż-żmien, kellhom ħafna iktar minn elf sena.

source: Vatican City's population is around 800. It is the smallest independent country in the world and the country with the lowest population.
reference: Il-popolazzjoni tal-Belt tal-Vatikan hija ta' madwar 800. Hija l-iżgħar pajjiż indipendenti fid-dinja u l-pajjiż bl-inqas popolazzjoni.

source: Vatican City uses Italian in its legislation and official communications.
reference: Il-Belt tal-Vatikan tuża t-Taljan fil-leġiżlazzjoni u l-komunikazzjonijiet uffiċjali tagħha.

source: Italian is also the everyday language used by most of those who work in the state while Latin is often used in religious ceremonies.
reference: Il-lingwa Taljana hija wkoll il-lingwa ta’ kuljum użata minn ħafna minn dawk li jaħdmu fl-istat filwaqt li l-Latin spiss jintuża f’ċerimonji reliġjużi.

source: All citizens of Vatican City are Roman Catholic.
reference: Iċ-ċittadini kollha tal-Belt tal-Vatikan huma Kattoliċi Rumani.

source: People have known about basic chemical elements such as gold, silver, and copper from antiquity, as these can all be discovered in nature in native form and are relatively simple to mine with primitive tools.
reference: In-nies ilhom jafu dwar elementi kimiċi bażiċi bħad-deheb, il-fidda u r-ram mill-antikità, peress li dawn kollha jistgħu jiġu skoperti fin-natura f’forma indiġena u huwa relattivament sempliċi li tħaffer għalihom fil-minjieri permezz ta’ għodod primittivi.

source: Aristotle, a philosopher, theorised that everything is made up of a mixture of one or more of four elements. They were earth, water, air, and fire.
reference: Aristotli, filosfu, ħareġ bit-teorija li kull ħaġa hija magħmula minn taħlita ta’ wieħed jew aktar minn erba’ elementi. Dawn kienu art, ilma, arja u nar.

source: This was more like the four states of matter (in the same order): solid, liquid, gas, and plasma, though he also theorised that they change into new substances to form what we see.
reference: Dan kien aktar bħall-erba' stati tal-materja (fl-istess ordni): solidu, likwidu, gass, u plażma, għalkemm huwa tteorizza wkoll li huma jinbidlu f'sustanzi ġodda biex jiffurmaw dak li naraw aħna.

source: Alloys are basically a mixture of two or more metals. Don't forget that there are many elements on the periodic table.
reference: Il-liga hija bażikament taħlita ta' żewġ metalli jew aktar. Tinsiex li hemm ħafna elementi fit-tabella perjodika.

source: Elements like calcium and potassium are considered metals. Of course, there are also metals like silver and gold.
reference: Elementi bħall-kalċju u l-potassju huma kkunsidrati metalli. Naturalment, hemm ukoll metalli bħall-fidda u d-deheb.

source: You can also have alloys that include small amounts of non-metallic elements like carbon.
reference: Jista' jkollok ukoll liga li tinkludi ammonti żgħar ta' elementi mhux metalliċi bħall-karbonju.

source: Everything in the Universe is made of matter. All matter is made of tiny particles called atoms.
reference: Kull ħaġa fl-Univers hija magħmula minn materja. Kull materja hija magħmula minn partiċelli żgħar ħafna li jissejħu atomi.

source: Atoms are so incredibly tiny that trillions of them could fit into the period at the end of this sentence.
reference: L-atomi huma tant inkredibbilment miniskoli li triljuni minnhom joqogħdu fil-punt fi tmiem din is-sentenza.

source: Thus, the pencil was a good friend to many people when it came out.
reference: Għalhekk, il-lapes kien ħabib tajjeb għal ħafna nies meta ħareġ.

source: Sadly, as newer methods of writing have emerged, the pencil has been relegated to lesser status and uses.
reference: Sfortunatament, hekk kif ħarġu metodi iktar ġodda ta’ kitba, il-lapes ġiet relegat għal status iktar baxx u inqas użu.

source: People now write messages on computer screens, never having to come close to a sharpener.
reference: In-nies issa jiktbu messaġġi fuq l-iskrins tal-kompjuter, u qatt ma jkollhom għalfejn jersqu lejn temprina.

source: One can only wonder what the keyboard will become when something newer comes along.
reference: Min jaf x’se ssir it-tastiera meta tiġi vvintata xi ħaġa iktar ġdida.

source: The fission bomb works on the principle that it takes energy to put together a nucleus with many protons and neutrons.
reference: Il-bomba tal-fissjoni taħdem fuq il-prinċipju li tittieħed l-enerġija biex nukleu jingħaqqad ma’ ħafna protoni u newtroni.

source: Sort of like rolling a heavy cart up a hill. Splitting the nucleus up again then releases some of that energy.
reference: B'mod simili bħal meta timbotta karrettun tqil fuq telgħa. Il-qsim tan-nukleu mill-ġdid imbagħad jirrilaxxa ftit minn dik l-enerġija.

source: Some atoms have unstable nuclei which means that they tend to break apart with little or no nudging.
reference: Xi atomi għandhom nuklei instabbli li jfisser li għandhom it-tendenza li jinqasmu bi ftit li xejn sforz.

source: The surface of the Moon is made of rocks and dust. The outer layer of the Moon is called the crust.
reference: Wiċċ il-Qamar huwa magħmul minn blat u trab. Is-saff ta' barra tal-Qamar jissejjaħ il-qoxra.

source: The crust is about 70 km thick on the near side and 100 km thick on the far side.
reference: Il-qoxra hija wiesgħa madwar 70 km fuq in-naħa l-iktar qrib u wiesgħa 100 km fuq in-naħa l-iktar ’il bogħod.

source: It is thinner under the maria and thicker under the highlands.
reference: Huwa irqaq taħt il-“maria” u eħxen taħt il-“highlands”.

source: There may be more maria on the near side because the crust is thinner. It was easier for lava to rise up to the surface.
reference: Jista' jkun hemm aktar ibħra tal-qamar fuq in-naħa viċina għax il-qoxra hija irqaq. Kienet aktar faċli għal-lava li titla' fil-wiċċ.

source: Content theories are centered on finding what makes people tick or appeals to them.
reference: It-teoriji tal-kontenut huma ċċentrati fuq is-sejba ta’ dak li jagħmel in-nies ferħanin jew jiġbidhom.

source: These theories suggest that people have certain needs and/or desires which have been internalized as they mature to adulthood.
reference: Dawn it-teoriji jissuġġerixxu li n-nies għandhom ċerti bżonnijiet u/jew xewqat li ġew internalizzati meta jimmaturaw għall-istat ta' adulti.

source: These theories look at what it is about certain people that make them want the things that they do and what things in their environment will make them do or not do certain things.
reference: Dawn it-teoriji janalizzaw x'inhu dak li lil ċerti nies iġegħlhom ikunu jridu l-affarijiet li jridu u liema affarijiet fl-ambjent tagħhom se jġegħluhom jagħmlu jew ma jagħmlux ċerti affarijiet.

source: Two popular content theories are Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs Theory and Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory.
reference: Żewġ teoriji tal-kontenut popolari huma t-Teorija tal-Ġerarkija tal-Ħtiġijiet ta' Maslow u t-Teorija taż-Żewġ Fatturi ta' Hertzberg.

source: Generally speaking, two behaviors can emerge as managers begin to lead their former peers. One end of the spectrum is trying to remain “one of the guys” (or gals).
reference: Ġeneralment, jistgħu jirriżultaw żewġ tipi ta’ attitudnijiet meta l-maniġers jibdew jimmaniġġjaw lil dawk li qabel kienu sħabhom. Tarf wieħed tal-ispettru huwa li wieħed jipprovaw jibqa’ jinħass bħala wieħed minnhom.

source: This type of manager has difficulty making unpopular decisions, performing disciplinary action, performance evaluations, assigning responsibility, and holding people accountable.
reference: Dan it-tip ta' maniġer isib diffikultà biex jieħu deċiżjonijiet mhux popolari, iwettaq azzjoni dixxiplinarja, iwettaq l-evalwazzjonijiet, jassenja r-responsabbiltajiet, u jara li n-nies ikunu responsabbli.

source: At the other end of the spectrum, one morphs into an unrecognizable individual that feels he or she must change everything the team has been doing and make it their own.
reference: Fin-naħa l-oħra tal-ispettru, wieħed jinbidel f’individwu li ma jintagħrafx li jħoss li għandu jbiddel kull għamel it-tim u jimmarkah bħala tiegħu.

source: After all, the leader is ultimately responsible for the success and failure of the team.
reference: Wara kollox, il-kap huwa finalment responsabbli għas-suċċess jew għall-falliment tat-tim.

source: This behavior oftentimes results in rifts between the leaders and the rest of the team.
reference: Din l-imġiba ħafna drabi toħloq firdiet bejn il-mexxejja u l-bqija tat-tim.

source: Virtual teams are held to the same standards of excellence as conventional teams, but there are subtle differences.
reference: Timijiet virtwali huma mistennija jissodisfaw l-istess standards ta' eċċellenza bħat-timijiet konvenzjonali, imma hemm xi differenzi sottili.

source: Virtual team members often function as the point of contact for their immediate physical group.
reference: Membri tat-tim virtwali ħafna drabi jiffunzjonaw bħala l-punt tal-kuntatt għall-grupp fiżiku immedjat tagħhom.

source: They often have more autonomy than conventional team members as their teams may meet according to varying time zones which may not be understood by their local management.
reference: Huma ħafna drabi għandhom aktar awtonomija minn membri ta' tim konvenzjonali peress li t-timijiet tagħhom jistgħu jiltaqgħu skont żoni tal-ħin li jvarjaw u dan jista' ma jinftiehemx mill-maniġment lokali tagħhom.

source: The presence of a true “invisible team” (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) is also a unique component of a virtual team.
reference: Il-preżenza ta' "tim inviżibbli" veru (Larson u LaFasto, 1989, paġna 109) hija wkoll komponent uniku ta' tim virtwali.

source: The “invisible team” is the management team to which each of the members report. The invisible team sets the standards for each member.
reference: It-“tim inviżibbli” huwa t-tim maniġerjali li kull wieħed mill-membri jirrapporta lilu. It-tim inviżibbli jistabbilixxi l-istandards għal kull membru.

source: Why would an organization want to go through the time consuming process of establishing a learning organization? One goal for putting organizational learning concepts into practice is innovation.
reference: Għaliex organizzazzjoni tkun trid tgħaddi mill-proċess li tant jieħu ħin ta' twaqqif ta' organizzazzjoni li titgħallem? Wieħed mill-għanijiet għat-twettiq fil-prattika tal-kunċetti ta' organizzazzjoni li titgħallem huwa l-innovazzjoni.

source: When all available resources are effectively used across the functional departments of an organization, creativity and ingenuity can transpire.
reference: Meta r-riżorsi disponibbli kollha jintużaw b'mod effettiv fid-dipartimenti funzjonali ta' organizzazzjoni, jistgħu joħorġu l-kreattività u l-inġenwità.

source: As a result, the process of an organization working together to overcome an obstacle can lead to a new innovative process to serve the customer's need.
reference: Bħala riżultat, il-proċess ta’ ħidma flimkien ta’ organizzazzjoni biex jiġi megħlub ostaklu jista’ jwassal għal proċess innovattiv ġdid li jissodisfa l-ħtiġijiet tal-klijent.

source: Before an organization can be innovative, leadership must create a culture of innovation as well as shared knowledge and organizational learning.
reference: Qabel organizzazzjoni tista’ tkun innovattiva, it-tmexxija trid toħloq kultura ta’ innovazzjoni kif ukoll għarfien kondiviż u tagħlim organizzattiv.

source: Angel (2006), explains the Continuum approach as a method being used to help organizations reach a higher level of performance.
reference: Angel (2006), jispjega l-approċċ tas-Sekwenza Kontinwa bħala metodu li qed jintuża biex jgħin lill-organizzazzjonijiet jilħqu livell ogħla ta' prestazzjoni.

source: Neurobiological data provide physical evidence for a theoretical approach to the investigation of cognition. Therefore it narrows the research area and makes it much more exact.
reference: Dejta newrobijoloġika tagħti prova fiżika tal-approċċ teoretiku għall-investigazzjoni tal-konjizzjoni. Għalhekk, dan jagħmel il-qasam tar-riċerka inqas vasta u jagħmilha ferm iktar preċiża.

source: The correlation between brain pathology and behaviour supports scientists in their research.
reference: Il-korrelazzjoni bejn il-patoloġija tal-moħħ u l-imġiba tappoġġja lix-xjenzati fir-riċerka tagħhom.

source: It has been known for a long time that different types of brain damage, traumas, lesions, and tumours affect behaviour and cause changes in some mental functions.
reference: Għal żmien twil kien magħruf li tipi differenti ta’ ħsara fil-moħħ, trawmi, leżjonijiet u tumuri jaffettwaw l-imġieba u jikkawżaw bidliet f’xi funzjonijiet mentali.

source: The rise of new technologies allows us to see and investigate brain structures and processes never seen before.
reference: L-iżvilupp ta' teknoloġiji ġodda jippermettilna li naraw u ninvestigaw l-istrutturi u l-proċessi tal-moħħ li qatt ma konna rajna qabel.

source: This provides us with a lot of information and material to build simulation models which help us to understand processes in our mind.
reference: Dan jipprovdilna ħafna informazzjoni u materjal għall-bini ta' mudelli ta' simulazzjoni li jgħinuna nifhmu l-proċessi f'moħħna.

source: Although AI has a strong connotation of science fiction, AI forms a very important branch of computer science, dealing with behavior, learning and intelligent adaptation in a machine.
reference: Għalkemm l-Intelliġenza Artifiċjali (AI) għandha konnottazzjoni qawwija ta' fantaxjenza, l-AI tifforma fergħa importanti ħafna tax-xjenza tal-kompjuter, u tittratta l-imġiba, it-tagħlim u l-adattament intelliġenti f'magna.

source: Research in AI involves making machines to automate tasks that require intelligent behavior.
reference: Ir-riċerka dwar l-IA tinvolvi l-ħolqien ta' magni li jawtomatizzaw kompiti li jeħtieġu mġiba intelliġenti.

source: Examples include control, planning and scheduling, the ability to answer customer diagnoses and questions, as well as handwriting recognition, voice and face.
reference: Eżempji jinkludu l-kontroll, l-ippjanar u l-iskedar, il-kapaċità li tingħata tweġiba lid-dijanjosi u l-mistoqsijiet tal-klijenti, kif ukoll ir-rikonoxximent tal-kalligrafija, il-vuċi u l-wiċċ.

source: Such things have become separate disciplines, which focus on providing solutions to real life problems.
reference: Affarijiet bħal dawn saru dixxiplini separati, li jiffokaw fuq is-sejbien ta’ soluzzjonijiet għal problemi tal-ħajja reali.

source: The AI ​​system is now often used in the fields of economics, medicine, engineering and the military, as has been built in several home computer and video game software applications.
reference: Is-sistema tal-IA issa spiss tintuża fl-oqsma tal-ekonomija, il-mediċina, l-inġinerija u l-militar, kif inbniet f’diversi applikazzjonijiet ta’ softwer tal-kompjuters fid-djar u vidjowgejms.

source: Field trips are a large part of any classroom. Quite often a teacher would love to take her students places to which a bus trip is not an option.
reference: Il-vjaġġi fuq il-post huma parti kbira minn kull klassi. Spiss, għalliema tħobb tieħu lill-istudenti tagħha f’postijiet li ma hemmx possibilità li tmur bil-karozza tal-linja fihom.

source: Technology offers the solution with virtual field trips. Students can look at museum artifacts, visit an aquarium, or admire beautiful art while sitting with their class.
reference: It-teknoloġija toffri s-soluzzjoni bi vjaġġi virtwali fuq il-post. L-istudenti jistgħu jħarsu lejn artefatti ta’ mużew, iżuru akkwarju, jew jammiraw arti sabiħa waqt li jkunu bilqiegħda mal-klassi tagħhom.

source: Sharing a field trip virtually is also a great way to reflect a on a trip and share experiences with future classes.
reference: Il-qsim ma’ ħaddieħor ta’ vjaġġ fuq il-post virtwalment huwa wkoll mod tajjeb ħafna kif tirrifletti fuq vjaġġ u taqsam esperjenzi ma’ klassijiet futuri.

source: For example, each year students from Bennet School in North Carolina design a website about their trip to the State Capital, each year the website gets remodeled, but old versions are kept online to serve as a scrapbook.
reference: Pereżempju, kull sena studenti mill-Iskola Bennet ġewwa North Carolina jiddisinjaw websajt dwar il-vjaġġ tagħhom lejn il-Kapital tal-Istat, kull sena l-websajt tiġi mibdula mill-ġdid, iżda l-verżjonijiet l-antiki jinżammu online biex iservu bħala scrapbook.

source: Blogs can also help improve student writing. While students often begin their blog experience with sloppy grammar and spelling, the presence of an audience generally changes that.
reference: Il-blogs jistgħu jgħinu wkoll biex titjieb il-kitba tal-istudenti. Għalkemm l-istudenti ħafna drabi jibdew l-esperjenza tagħhom tal-blogs bi grammatika u ortografija mhux tajba, il-fatt li jkollhom udjenza ġeneralment iwassal biex din is-sitwazzjoni tinbidel.

source: Since students are often the most critical audience, the blog writer begins to strive to improve writing to avoid criticism.
reference: Peress li l-istudenti ħafna drabi huma l-iktar udjenza kritika, il-kittieb tal-blog jibda jaħdem biex itejjeb il-kitba ħalli ma jiġix ikkritikat.

source: Also blogging "forces students to become more savvy about the world around them." The need to feed the interest of the audience inspires students to be clever and interesting (Toto, 2004).
reference: Ukoll il-blogging "iġiegħel lill-istudenti jsiru jafu aktar dwar id-dinja ta' madwarhom." Il-bżonn li jissodisfaw l-interess tal-udjenza jispira lill-istudenti biex ikunu aktar jilħqilhom u interessanti (Toto, 2004).

source: Blogging is a tool that inspires collaboration, and encourages students to extend learning well beyond the traditional school day.
reference: Il-blogging huwa għodda li tispira l-kollaborazzjoni, u tħeġġeġ lill-istudenti jestendu t-tagħlim tagħhom ferm lil hinn mill-ġurnata tradizzjonali tal-iskola.

source: Appropriate use of blogs "can empower students to become more analytical and critical; through actively responding to Internet materials, students can define their positions in the context of others' writings as well as outline their own perspectives on particular issues (Oravec, 2002).
reference: L-użu xieraq tal-blogs "jista’ jagħti s-setgħa lill-istudenti biex isiru iktar analitiċi u kritiċi; billi jirrispondu b’mod attiv għal materjali tal-Internet, l-istudenti jkunu jistgħu jiddefinixxu l-pożizzjonijiet tagħhom fil-kuntest ta’ kitbiet ta’ persuni oħrajn u jispjegaw il-perspettivi tagħhom dwar kwistjonijiet partikolari (Oravec, 2002).

source: Ottawa is Canada's charming, bilingual capital and features an array of art galleries and museums that showcase Canada's past and present.
reference: Ottawa hija l-belt kapitali inkantevoli u bilingwi tal-Kanada u fiha firxa ta’ galleriji tal-arti u mużewijiet li juru l-passat u l-preżent tal-Kanada.

source: Farther south is Niagara Falls and the north is home to the untapped natural beauty of the Muskoka and beyond.
reference: Aktar lejn in-Nofsinhar hemm in-Niagara Falls u lejn it-Tramuntana hemm is-sbuħija naturali mhux sfruttata tal-Muskoka u lil hinn minnha.

source: All these things and more highlight Ontario as what is considered quintessentially Canadian by outsiders.
reference: Dawn l-affarijiet kollha u aktar jenfasizzaw lil Ontario bħala l-eżempju perfett ta' dak li jitqies bħala Kanadiż mill-barranin.

source: Large areas further north are quite sparsely populated and some is nearly uninhabited wilderness.
reference: Inħawi kbar iktar lejn it-Tramuntana għandhom popolazzjoni pjuttost skarsa u xi wħud huma nħawi fi stat naturali kważi diżabitati.

source: For a comparison of population that surprises many: There are more African Americans living in the US than there are Canadian citizens.
reference: Għal paragun tal-popolazzjoni li tissorprendi lil bosta nies: Hemm aktar Afrikani Amerikani jgħixu fl-Istati Uniti milli hemm ċittadini Kanadiżi.

source: The East African Islands are in the Indian Ocean off the eastern coast of Africa.
reference: Il-Gżejjer tal-Afrika tal-Lvant jinsabu fl-Oċean Indjan barra mill-kosta tal-Lvant tal-Afrika.

source: Madagascar is by far the biggest, and a continent on its own when it comes to wildlife.
reference: Madagascar huwa bil-bosta l-ikbar wieħed, u kontinent għalih meta nitkellmu dwar annimali selvaġġi.

source: Most of the smaller islands are independent nations, or associated with France, and known as luxury beach resorts.
reference: Ħafna mill-gżejjer iż-żgħar huma nazzjonijiet indipendenti, jew assoċjati ma’ Franza, u magħrufin bħala resorts ta’ lussu fuq bajjiet.

source: The Arabs also brought Islam to the lands, and it took in a big way in the Comoros and Mayotte.
reference: L-Għarab ġabu wkoll l-Iżlam fl-artijiet, u dan inxtered bil-kbir fil-Komoros u l-Mayotte.

source: European influence and colonialism began in the 15th century, as Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama found the Cape Route from Europe to India.
reference: L-influwenza Ewropea u l-kolonjaliżmu bdew fis-seklu 15, hekk kif l-esploratur Portugiż Vasco da Gama sab il-Cape Route mill-Ewropa għall-Indja.

source: In the north the region is bounded by the Sahel, and in the south and west by the Atlantic Ocean.
reference: Fit-Tramuntana, ir-reġjun huwa mdawwar bis-Sahel, u fin-Nofsinhar u fil-Punent bl-Oċean Atlantiku.

source: Women: It is recommended that any women travellers say that they are married, regardless of actual marital status.
reference: Nisa: Huwa rrakkomandat li kwalunkwe mara li qed tivvjaġġa tgħid li hija miżżewġa, hu x'inhu l-istat attwali tagħha rigward iż-żwieġ.

source: It is helpful to also wear a ring (just not one that looks too expensive.
reference: Huwa utli li tilbes ċurkett ukoll (biss mhux wieħed li jidher li jiswa ħafna flus.

source: Women should realize that cultural differences may result in what they would consider harassment and it is not uncommon to be followed, grabbed by the arm, etc.
reference: In-nisa għandhom jifhmu li d-differenzi kulturali jistgħu jirriżultaw f'dak li huma jistgħu jqisuh bħala fastidju u mhijiex ħaġa rari li jiġu segwiti, maħtufin mid-driegħ, eċċ.

source: Be firm in turning down men, and don't be afraid to stand your ground (cultural differences or not, it doesn't make it ok!).
reference: Żomm sod meta tirrifjuta lill-irġiel, u tibżax tinsisti fuq il-preferenza tiegħek (kemm jekk hemm differenzi kulturali jew le, ma jfissirx li trid taċċetta kollox bħala tajjeb!).

source: The modern city of Casablanca was founded by Berber fishermen in the 10th century BCE, and was used by the Phoenicians, Romans, and the Merenids as a strategic port called Anfa.
reference: Il-belt moderna ta’ Casablanca twaqqfet minn sajjieda Berberi fis-seklu 10 Q.E.K., u l-Feniċi, ir-Rumani, u l-Merenidi użawha bħala port strateġiku msejjaħ Anfa.

source: The Portuguese destroyed it and rebuilt it under the name Casa Branca, only to abandon it after an earthquake in 1755.
reference: Il-Portugiżi kissruha u reġgħu bnewha taħt l-isem ta' Casa Branca, imma mbagħad abbandunawha wara terremot fl-1755.

source: The Moroccan sultan rebuilt the city as Daru l-Badya and it was given the name Casablanca by Spanish traders who established trading bases there.
reference: Is-sultan Marokkin reġa’ bena l-belt bħala Daru l-Badya u ngħatat l-isem “Casablanca” minn negozjanti Spanjoli li stabbilixxew bażijiet ta’ kummerċ hemmhekk.

source: Casablanca is one of the least interesting places to shop in all of Morocco.
reference: Casablanca huwa wieħed mill-inqas postijiet interessanti biex tixtri fil-Marokk kollu.

source: Around the old Medina it's easy to find places selling traditional Moroccan goods, such as tagines, pottery, leather goods, hookahs, and a whole spectrum of geegaws, but it's all for the tourists.
reference: Madwar il-Medina l-antika, faċli ssib postijiet li jbigħu oġġetti tradizzjonali tal-Marokk, bħal tagines, oġġetti tal-fuħħar, oġġetti tal-ġilda, hookahs, u spettru sħiħ ta’ geegaws, iżda kollha huma turistiċi.

source: Goma is a tourist city of the Democratic Republic of Congo in the extreme east near Rwanda.
reference: Goma hija belt turistika tar-Repubblika Demokratika tal-Kongo fil-Lvant estrem qrib ir-Rwanda.

source: In 2002 Goma was destroyed by lava from the Nyiragongo volcano which buried most of the town’s streets, particularly the town centre.
reference: Fl-2002, Goma nqerdet b'lava mill-vulkan Nyiragongo li difen il-maġġor parti tat-toroq tal-belt, b'mod partikolari ċ-ċentru tal-belt.

source: While Goma is reasonably safe, any visits outside of Goma should be researched to understand the state of the fighting that persists in the North Kivu province.
reference: Waqt li Goma hija raġonevolment sikura, kwalunkwe żjara 'l barra minn Goma għandha tiġi rriċerkata biex ikun magħruf l-istat tal-ġlied li għadu jippersisti fil-provinċja ta' Kivu tat-Tramuntana.

source: The city is also the base to climb the Nyiragongo volcano along with some of the cheapest Mountain Gorilla tracking in Africa.
reference: Il-belt hija wkoll il-bażi biex tixxabbat mal-vulkan Nyiragongo flimkien ma’ ftit mill-irħas trekking tal-Gorilla tal-Muntanji fl-Afrika.

source: You can use boda-boda (motorcycle taxi) to get around Goma. The normal (local) price is ~500 Congolese Francs for the short ride.
reference: Inti tista' tuża l-bodaboda (mutur taksi) biex iddur ġo Goma. Il-prezz (lokali) normali huwa ~500 Frank Kongoliż għal dawra qasira.

source: Combined with its relative inaccessibility, "Timbuktu" has come to be used as a metaphor for exotic, distant lands.
reference: Flimkien man-nuqqas ta’ aċċessibbiltà relattiva tagħha, "Timbuktu" ntużat bħala metafora għal artijiet eżotiċi u mbiegħda.

source: Today, Timbuktu is an impoverished town, although its reputation makes it a tourist attraction, and it has an airport.
reference: Illum, Timbuktu hija belt imfaqqra, għalkemm ir-reputazzjoni tagħha tagħmilha attrazzjoni turistika, u għandha ajruport.

source: In 1990, it was added to the list of world heritage sites in danger, due to the threat of desert sands.
reference: Fl-1990, ġie miżjud mal-lista ta’ siti tal-wirt dinji fil-periklu, minħabba t-theddida ta’ ramel tad-deżert.

source: It was one of the major stops during Henry Louis Gates' PBS special Wonders of the African World.
reference: Kienet waħda mill-waqfiet ewlenin waqt il-sensiela speċjali tal-PBS “Wonders of the African World” ta’ Henry Louis Gates.

source: The city is in stark contrast to the rest of the country's cities, because it has more of an Arabic flair than of an African.
reference: Il-belt tikkuntrasta b’mod qawwi mal-bliet l-oħra tal-pajjiż, minħabba li għandha elementi iktar Għarbin milli Afrikani.

source: The Kruger National Park (KNP) lies in the north-east of South Africa and runs along the border of Mozambique in the east, Zimbabwe in the north, and the southern border is the Crocodile River.
reference: Il-Park Nazzjonali Kruger (KNP) jinsab fil-Grigal tal-Afrika t’Isfel u jgħaddi tul il-fruntiera tal-Mozambique fil-Lvant, iż-Żimbabwe fit-Tramuntana, u l-fruntiera tan-Nofsinhar hija x-Xmara tal-Kukkudrilli (“Crocodile River”).

source: The park covers 19,500 km² and is divided in 14 different ecozones, each supporting different wildlife.
reference: Il-park huwa mifrux fuq 19,500 km² u huwa maqsum f'14-il ekożona differenti, u kull waħda fiha annimali selvaġġi differenti.

source: It is one of the main attractions of South Africa and it is considered the flagship of South African National Parks (SANParks).
reference: Huwa wieħed mill-attrazzjonijiet ewlenin tal-Afrika t’Isfel u huwa meqjus bħala x-xempju tal-Parks Nazzjonali tal-Afrika t’Isfel (“South African National Parks” - SANParks).

source: As with all South African National Parks, there are daily conservation and entry fees for the park.
reference: Bħal fil-każ tal-Parkijiet Nazzjonali kollha tal-Afrika t’Isfel, jiġu applikati miżati ta’ kuljum għall-konservazzjoni u d-dħul fil-park.

source: It may also be beneficial for one to buy a Wild Card, which provides entry to either selections of parks in South Africa or all of the South African National Parks.
reference: Jista’ jkun ta’ benefiċċju wkoll li wieħed jixtri Wild Card, li tipprovdi dħul jew għal selezzjonijiet ta’ parks fl-Afrika t’Isfel jew għall-Parks Nazzjonali kollha tal-Afrika t’Isfel.

source: Hong Kong Island gives the territory of Hong Kong its name and is the place that many tourists regard as the main focus.
reference: Il-Gżira ta’ Ħong Kong (“Hong Kong Island”) tagħti isimha lit-territorju ta’ Ħong Kong u hija l-post li bosta turisti jqisuh bħala l-fokus ewlieni.

source: The parade of buildings that make the Hong Kong skyline has been likened to a glittering bar chart that is made apparent by the presence of the waters of Victoria Harbour.
reference: L-iskjerament tal-bini li jifforma l-iskajlajn ta' Ħong Kong ġie mxebbaħ ma' bar ċart b'dawl ileqq minħabba kif jidher bil-preżenza tal-ilmijiet tal-Port ta' Victoria.

source: To get the best views of Hong Kong, leave the island and head for the Kowloon waterfront opposite.
reference: Biex ikollok l-aqwa veduti ta' Ħong Kong, itlaq mill-gżira u mur il-waterfront ta' Kowloon fin-naħa l-oħra.

source: The great majority of Hong Kong Island's urban development is densely packed on reclaimed land along the northern shore.
reference: Il-maġġor parti tal-iżvilupp urban tal-Gżira ta’ Hong Kong hija ppakkjata b’mod dens fuq art reklamata max-xatt tat-Tramuntana.

source: This is the place the British colonisers took as their own and so if you are looking for evidence of the territory's colonial past, this is a good place to start.
reference: Dan huwa l-post li ħadu l-kolonizzaturi Brittaniċi bħala tagħhom u għalhekk jekk qed tfittex evidenza tal-passat kolonjali tat-territorju, dan huwa post tajjeb fejn tibda.

source: The Sundarbans are the largest littoral mangrove belt in the world, stretching 80 km (50 mi) into the Bangladeshi and Indian hinterland from the coast.
reference: Is-Sundarbans huma l-akbar foresta tal-mangrovja littorali fid-dinja, twila 80 km (50 mil) mill-kosta sal-intern tal-Bangladexx u l-Indja.

source: The Sundarbans has been declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The part of the forest within Indian territory is called Sundarbans National Park.
reference: Is-Sundarbans ġew iddikjarati bħala Sit ta' Wirt Dinji tal-UNESCO. Il-parti tal-foresta fit-territorju Indjan tissejjaħ il-Park Nazzjonali tas-Sundarbans.

source: The forests aren't just mangrove swamps though — they include some of the last remaining stands of the mighty jungles which once covered the Gangetic plain.
reference: Il-foresti mhumiex biss mangrovji f’artijiet mistagħdra — huma jinkludu xi wħud mill-aħħar pożizzjonijiet li għad fadal tal-ġungli imponenti li xi darba kienu jkopru l-pjanura Ganġetika.

source: The Sundarbans cover an area of 3,850 km², of which about one-third is covered in water/marsh areas.
reference: Is-Sundarbans ikopru żona ta' 3,850 km², li madwar terz minnha hija miksija bl-ilma/żoni mistagħdra.

source: Since 1966 the Sundarbans have been a wildlife sanctuary, and it is estimated that there are now 400 Royal Bengal tigers and about 30,000 spotted deer in the area.
reference: Sa mill-1966 is-Sundarbans kienu santwarju tal-organiżmi selvaġġi, u huwa stmat li bħalissa hemm 400 tigra tal-Bengal Irjali u madwar 30,000 ċriev bit-tikek fiż-żona.

source: Buses depart the inter-district bus station (across the river) throughout the day, though most, especially those heading to the east and Jakar/Bumthang leave between 06:30 and 07:30.
reference: Il-karozzi tal-linja jitilqu mill-istazzjon tal-karozzi tal-linja bejn id-distretti (tul ix-xmara) matul il-jum, għalkemm ħafna minnhom, speċjalment dawk li sejrin lejn il-Lvant u Jakar/Bumthang jitilqu bejn is-6:30 u s-7:30 ta’ filgħodu.

source: As the inter-district buses are often full, it is advisable to purchase a ticket a few days in advance.
reference: Peress li l-karozzi tal-linja bejn id-distretti ħafna drabi jkunu mimlijin, huwa rakkomandat li l-biljett tixtrih ftit ġranet minn qabel.

source: Most districts are served by small Japanese Coaster Buses, which are comfortable and sturdy.
reference: Bosta distretti fihom servizzi ta' Coaster Buses Ġappuniżi żgħar, li huma komdi u sodi.

source: Shared taxis are a quick and comfortable means to travel to nearby places, such as Paro (Nu 150) and Punakha (Nu 200).
reference: Taksis maqsumin ma’ ħaddieħor huma mezz veloċi u komdu kif tivvjaġġa lejn postijiet fil-qrib, bħal Paro (Nu 150) u Punakha (Nu 200).

source: The Oyapock River Bridge is a cable-stayed bridge. It spans the Oyapock River to link the cities of Oiapoque in Brazil and Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock in French Guiana.
reference: Il-Pont tax-Xmara Oyapock huwa pont miżmum bil-kejbils. Huwa jaqsam ix-Xmara Oyapock biex jgħaqqad il-bliet ta' Oiapoque fil-Brażil u Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock fil-Guyana Franċiża.

source: The two towers rise to a height of 83 meters, it's 378 meters long and it has two lanes of 3.50 m wide.
reference: Iż-żewġ torrijiet huma għoljin 83 metru, huwa twil 378 metru u għandu żewġ karreġġati wisgħin 3.50 m.

source: The vertical clearance under the bridge is 15 meters. Construction was completed in August 2011, it didn't open to traffic until March 2017.
reference: Il-vojt vertikali taħt il-pont huwa ta' 15-il metru. Il-kostruzzjoni tlestiet f'Awwissu tal-2011, ma fetaħx għat-traffiku qabel Marzu tal-2017.

source: The bridge is scheduled to be fully operational in September 2017, when the Brazilian customs checkpoints are expected to be finished.
reference: Il-pont huwa skedat li jkun kompletament operattiv f’Settembru tal-2017, meta huma mistennija li jkunu lesti l-punti ta’ kontroll doganali Brażiljani.

source: The Guaraní were the most significant indigenous group inhabiting what is now Eastern Paraguay, living as semi-nomadic hunters who also practised subsistence agriculture.
reference: Il-Guaraní kienu l-iktar grupp indiġenu sinifikanti li jgħix f’dak li llum huwa l-Paragwaj tal-Lvant, li jgħixu bħala kaċċaturi semi-nomadiċi li pprattikaw ukoll l-agrikoltura ta’ sussistenza.

source: The Chaco region was home to other groups of indigenous tribes such as the Guaycurú and Payaguá, who survived by hunting, gathering and fishing.
reference: Ir-reġjun Chaco kien fih gruppi oħra ta’ tribujiet indiġeni bħall-Guaycurú u Payaguá, li baqgħu ħajjin bil-kaċċa, il-ħsad u s-sajd.

source: In the 16th century Paraguay, formerly called "The Giant Province of the Indies", was born as a result of the encounter of Spanish conquerors with the native indigenous groups.
reference: Fis-seklu 16, il-Paragwaj, li qabel kienet tissejjaħ "Il-Provinzja Ġganta tal-Indji", twieldet bħala riżultat tal-laqgħa tal-konkwistaturi Spanjoli mal-gruppi indiġeni nattivi.

source: The Spaniards started the colonization period which lasted for three centuries.
reference: L-Ispanjoli bdew il-perjodu tal-kolonizzazzjoni li dam tliet sekli.

source: Since the foundation of Asunción in 1537, Paraguay has managed to keep a lot of its indigenous character and identity.
reference: Sa mill-fondazzjoni ta’ Asunción fl-1537, il-Paragwaj irnexxielu jżomm ħafna mill-karattru u l-identità indiġena tiegħu.

source: Argentina is well known for having one of the best polo teams and players in the world.
reference: L-Arġentina hija magħrufa bħala li għandha wieħed mill-aqwa timijiet u plejers tal-polo fid-dinja.

source: The largest tournament of the year takes place in December at the polo fields in Las Cañitas.
reference: L-ikbar turnament tas-sena jsir f’Diċembru fil-kampijiet tal-polo ġewwa Las Cañitas.

source: Smaller tournaments and matches can also be seen here at other times of the year.
reference: Turnamenti u logħbiet iżgħar jistgħu jidhru hawn ukoll fi żminijiet oħra tas-sena.

source: For news on tournaments and where to buy tickets for polo matches, check Asociacion Argentina de Polo.
reference: Għal aħbarijiet dwar tournaments u fejn tixtri biljetti għal-logħbiet tal-polo, iċċekkja l-Asociacion Argentina de Polo.

source: The official Falklands currency is the Falkland pound (FKP) whose value is set equivalent to that of one British pound (GBP).
reference: Il-munita uffiċjali tal-Falklands hija l-lira Falklandiża (FKP) li l-valur tagħha huwa ekwivalenti għal dak tal-lira Brittanika (GBP).

source: Money can be exchanged at the only bank in the islands which is located in Stanley across from the FIC West store.
reference: Il-flus jistgħu jiġu skambjati fl-uniku bank fil-gżejjer li jinsab fi Stanley biswit FIC West store.

source: British pounds will generally be accepted anywhere in the islands and within Stanley credit cards and United States dollars are also often accepted.
reference: Il-liri Ingliżi ġeneralment se jiġu aċċettati kullimkien fil-gżejjer u fi Stanley il-karti ta’ kreditu u d-dollari Amerikani huma wkoll aċċettati spiss.

source: On the outlying islands credit cards will probably not be accepted, although British and United States currency may be taken; check with the owners in advance to determine what is an acceptable payment method.
reference: Fuq il-gżejjer esterni, il-karti ta’ kreditu probabbli mhux se jiġu aċċettati, għalkemm jista' jkun li l-munita Ingliża u dik tal-Istati Uniti jiġu aċċettati; kellem lis-sidien bil-quddiem biex tara x’inhu metodu ta’ ħlas aċċettabbli.

source: It is nearly impossible to exchange Falklands currency outside of the islands, so exchange money prior to leaving the islands.
reference: Kważi impossibbli tibdel il-munita tal-Falklands barra mill-gżejjer, mela skambja l-flus qabel titlaq mill-gżejjer.

source: Since Montevideo is south of the Equator, it is summer there when it's winter in the Northern Hemisphere and vice versa.
reference: Peress li Montevideo tinsab fin-Nofsinhar tal-Ekwatur, hemm ikun is-sajf meta fl-Emisferu tat-Tramuntana tkun ix-xitwa u viċi versa.

source: Montevideo is in the subtropics; in the summer months, temperatures above +30°C are common.
reference: Montevideo jinsab fis-subtropiċi; fix-xhur tas-sajf, temperaturi ’l fuq minn +30°C huma komuni.

source: The winter can be deceptively chilly: temperatures rarely go below freezing, but the wind and humidity combine to make it feel colder than what the thermometer says.
reference: Ix-xitwa tista' tkun kiesħa b'mod qarrieq: it-temperaturi rari jinżlu taħt l-iffriżar, imma r-riħ u l-umdità jingħaqdu biex tinħass aktar kesħa minn dik li jindika t-termometru.

source: There are no particular "rainy" and "dry" seasons: the amount of rain stays roughly the same throughout the year.
reference: M'hemmx staġuni partikulari "bix-xita" u "nixfin": l-ammont tax-xita jibqa' bejn wieħed u ieħor l-istess tul is-sena kollha.

source: Though many of the animals in the park are used to seeing humans, the wildlife is nonetheless wild and should not be fed or disturbed.
reference: Għalkemm bosta annimali fil-park imdorrijin jaraw bnedmin, l-annimali selvaġġi xorta jibgħu selvaġġi u m'għandhomx jingħataw ikel jew jiġu ddisturbati.

source: According to park authorities, stay at least 100 yards/meters away from bears and wolves and 25 yards/meters from all other wild animals!
reference: Skont l-awtoritajiet tal-park, żomm mill-inqas 100 jarda/metru 'l bogħod mill-orsijiet u l-lupi u 25 jarda/metru 'l bogħod mill-annimali selvaġġi l-oħra kollha!

source: No matter how docile they may look, bison, elk, moose, bears, and nearly all large animals can attack.
reference: Indipendentement minn kemm jistgħu jidhru doċli, il-biżonti, l-elk, il-moose (ċriev kbar tal-Amerika tat-Tramuntana), l-orsijiet, u kważi l-annimali kollha li huma kbar jistgħu jattakkaw.

source: Each year, dozens of visitors are injured because they didn't keep a proper distance. These animals are large, wild, and potentially dangerous, so give them their space.
reference: Kull sena, għexieren ta’ viżitaturi jkunu midruba minħabba li ma jżommux id-distanza xierqa. Dawn l-annimali huma kbar, slavaġ, u potenzjalment perikolużi, għalhekk tihom l-ispazju tagħhom.

source: In addition, be aware that odors attract bears and other wildlife, so avoid carrying or cooking odorous foods and keep a clean camp.
reference: Madankollu, għandek tkun taf li l-irwejjaħ jiġbdu lill-orsijiet u annimali selvaġġi oħra, mela evita li ġġorr jew issajjar ikel b’irwejjaħ qawwijin u żomm il-kamp nadif.

source: Apia is the capital of Samoa. The town is on the island of Upolu and has a population of just under 40,000.
reference: Apia hija l-kapitali tas-Samoa. Din il-belt qiegħda fuq il-gżira ta' Upolu u għandha popolazzjoni ta' ftit inqas minn 40,000.

source: Apia was founded in the 1850s and has been the official capital of Samoa since 1959.
reference: Apia twaqqfet f'nofs is-seklu dsatax u ilha l-kapitali uffiċjali tas-Samoa mill-1959.

source: The harbor was the site of an infamous naval standoff in 1889 when seven ships from Germany, the US, and Britain refused to leave the harbor.
reference: Il-port kien il-post fejn sar il-kunflitt infami navali fl-1889 fejn seba’ vapuri mill-Ġermanja, l-Istati Uniti u l-Gran Brittanja rrifjutaw li jħallu l-port.

source: All the ships were sunk, except for one British cruiser. Nearly 200 American and German lives were lost.
reference: Il-vapuri kollha ġew mgħarrqa, minbarra krużer Brittaniku wieħed. Kważi 200 Amerikan u Ġermaniż tilfu ħajjithom.

source: During the struggle for independence organised by the Mau movement, a peaceful gathering in the town resulted in the killing of the paramount chief Tupua Tamasese Lealofi III.
reference: Matul il-ġlieda għall-indipendenza organizzata mill-moviment Mau, laqgħa paċifika fil-belt irriżultat fil-qtil tal-kap ewlieni Tupua Tamasese Lealofi III.

source: There are many beaches, due to Auckland's straddling of two harbours. The most popular ones are in three areas.
reference: Hemm ħafna xtut bir-ramel, minħabba li Auckland qiegħda bejn żewġ portijiet. Dawk l-aktar magħrufa jinsabu fi tliet żoni.

source: North Shore beaches (in North Harbour district) are on the Pacific Ocean and stretch from Long Bay in the north to Devonport in the south.
reference: Il-bajjiet tan-North Shore (fid-distrett ta’ North Harbour) jinsabu f-Oċean Paċifiku u jibdew minn Long Bay fit-Tramuntana sa Devonport fin-Nofsinhar.

source: They are almost all sandy beaches with safe swimming, and most have shade provided by pohutukawa trees.
reference: Huma kważi kollha xtut bir-ramel b'għawm sikur, u l-maġġoranza għandhom id-dell ipprovdut mis-siġar tal-pohutukawa.

source: Tamaki Drive beaches are on the Waitemata Harbour, in the upmarket suburbs of Mission Bay and St Heliers in Central Auckland.
reference: Il-bajjiet ta’ Tamaki Drive jinsabu ġewwa l-Port ta’ Waitemata, fis-subborgi prestiġjużi ta’ Mission Bay u St Heliers fiċ-Ċentru ta’ Auckland.

source: These are sometimes-crowded family beaches with a good range of shops lining the shore. Swimming is safe.
reference: Dawn huma xtut bir-ramel għall-familja li kultant ikunu ffullati u jkollhom firxa tajba ta' ħwienet matul ix-xatt. L-għawm huwa sikur.

source: The main local beer is 'Number One', it is not a complex beer, but pleasant and refreshing. The other local beer is called "Manta".
reference: Il-birra lokali ewlenija hija “Number One”, mhix birra kumplessa, iżda hija pjaċevoli u rinfreskanti. Il-birra lokali l-oħra msejħa "Manta".

source: There are many French wines to be had, but the New Zealand and Australian wines might travel better.
reference: Hemm ħafna nbejjed Franċiżi li tista' ssib, imma l-inbejjed ta' New Zealand u tal-Awstralja probabbilment jivvjaġġaw aħjar.

source: The local tap water is perfectly safe to drink, but bottled water is easy to find if you are fearful.
reference: L-ilma tal-vit lokali tajjeb għax-xorb, imma faċilment issib ilma bbottiljat jekk għandek xi tħassib.

source: For Australians, the idea of 'flat white' coffee is foreign. A short black is 'espresso', cappuccino comes heaped high with cream (not froth), and tea is served without milk.
reference: Għall-Awstraljani, l-idea tal-kafè msejjaħ “flat white” mhix familjari. “Short black” huwa “espresso”, kapuċċino jiġi mimli b’ħafna krema (mhux ragħwa), u t-te huwa servut mingħajr ħalib.

source: The hot chocolate is up to Belgian standards. Fruit juices are pricey but excellent.
reference: Iċ-ċikkulata sħuna hija skont l-istandards Belġjani. Il-meraq tal-frott jiswa ħafna flus iżda huwa eċċellenti.

source: Many trips to the reef are made all year around, and injuries due to any of these causes on the reef are rare.
reference: Ħafna vjaġġi lejn is-sikka jsiru s-sena kollha, u l-korrimenti minħabba xi waħda minn dawn il-kawżi fuq is-sikka huma rari.

source: Still, take advice from authorities, obey all signs, and pay close attention to safety warnings.
reference: Xorta, għandek tieħu parir mingħand l-awtoritajiet, timxi mas-sinjali kollha, u toqgħod attent/a ħafna għall-messaġġi tas-sigurtà.

source: Box jellyfish occur near beaches and near river estuaries from October to April north of 1770. They can occasionally be found outside these times.
reference: Il-bram forma ta’ kaxxa (“box jellyfish”) jinstab ħdejn il-bajjiet u ħdejn l-estwarji tax-xmajjar minn Ottubru sa April fit-tramuntana tal-1770. Kultant wieħed jista’ jarahom ukoll barra minn dawn iż-żminijiet.

source: Sharks do exist, however they rarely attack humans. Most sharks are scared of humans and would swim away.
reference: Għalkemm il-klieb il-baħar jeżistu, rari jattakkaw lin-nies. Ħafna mill-klieb il-baħar jibżgħu min-nies u jgħumu ’l bogħod minnhom.

source: Saltwater Crocodiles do not actively live in the ocean, their primary habitat is in river estuaries north from Rockhampton.
reference: Il-Kukkudrilli tal-Ilma Mielaħ ma jgħixux attivament fl-oċean, l-abitat primarju tagħhom huwa fl-estwarji tax-xmajjar fit-Tramuntana minn Rockhampton.

source: Booking in advance gives the traveller peace of mind that they will have somewhere to sleep once they arrive at their destination.
reference: Jekk jibbukkja minn qabel, il-vjaġġatur ikollu moħħu mistrieħ li se jkollu x’imkien fejn jorqod malli jasal fid-destinazzjoni tiegħu.

source: Travel agents often have deals with specific hotels, although you may find it possible to book other forms of accommodation, like camping grounds, through a travel agent.
reference: L-aġenti tal-ivvjaġġar ħafna drabi jagħmlu ftehimiet ma' lukandi speċifiċi, għalkemm inti tista' ssibha possibbli li tibbukkja forom oħra ta' akkomodazzjoni, bħal postijiet tal-ikkampjar, permezz ta' aġent tal-ivvjaġġar.

source: Travel agents usually offer packages that include breakfast, transportation arrangements to/from the airport or even combined flight and hotel packages.
reference: L-aġenti tal-ivvjaġġar ħafna drabi joffru pakketti li jinkludu l-brekfast, arranġamenti tat-trasport għal/minn l-ajruport u anki pakketti li jikkombinaw it-titjira u l-lukanda.

source: They can also hold the reservation for you if you need time to think about the offer or procure other documents for your destination (e.g. visa).
reference: Huma jistgħu jżommu wkoll ir-reservation għalik jekk teħtieġ aktar żmien biex taħseb dwar l-offerta jew biex tikseb dokumenti oħra għad-destinazzjoni tiegħek (eż. viża).

source: Any amendments or requests though should be coursed through the travel agent first and not directly with the hotel.
reference: Kwalunkwe emenda jew talba pero għandha tgħaddi għand l-aġent tal-ivvjaġġar l-ewwel u mhux direttament għand il-lukanda.

source: For some festivals, the vast majority of the attendants to music festivals decide to camp on site, and most attendants consider it a vital part of the experience.
reference: Għal xi festivals, il-maġġoranza l-kbira ta’ dawk li jmorru festivals tal-mużika jiddeċiedu li jikkampjaw fuq il-post, u ħafna minnhom jqisu li dan huwa parti essenzjali mill-esperjenza.

source: If you want to be close to the action you're going to have to get in early to get a camping site close to the music.
reference: Jekk trid tara l-azzjoni mill-qrib trid tasal kmieni biex ikollok sit tal-kampeġġ qrib il-mużika.

source: Remember that even though music on the main stages may have finished, there may be sections of the festival that will keep playing music until late into the night.
reference: Ftakar li għalkemm il-mużika fuq il-palkijiet ewlenin tista’ tkun spiċċat, jista’ jkun hemm sezzjonijiet tal-festival li jibqgħu jdoqqu l-mużika sa tard bil-lejl.

source: Some festivals have special camping areas for families with young children.
reference: Xi festivals għandhom żoni ta’ kampeġġ speċjali għal familji bi tfal żgħar.

source: If crossing the Northern Baltic in winter, check the cabin location, as going through ice causes quite horrible noise for those most affected.
reference: Jekk tkun se taqsam il-Baltiku tat-Tramuntana fix-xitwa, ara sew il-post tal-kabina, minħabba li meta tgħaddu mis-silġ joħroġ ħoss orribbli għal dawk l-iktar affettwati.

source: Saint Petersburg cruises include time in town. Cruise passengers are exempted from visa requirements (check the terms).
reference: Il-kruċieri f’San Pietruburgu jinkludu ħin fil-belt. Il-passiġġieri tal-kruċieri huma eżentati mir-rekwiżiti tal-viża (iċċekkja t-termini).

source: Casinos typically make many efforts to maximize time and money spent by guests. Windows and clocks are usually absent, and exits can be hard to find.
reference: Il-każinos tipikament jagħmlu sforzi kbar biex jimmassimizzaw il-ħin u l-flus li jonfqu fih il-mistednin. Ġeneralment ma jkunx hemm twieqi u arloġġi u jista’ jkun diffiċli biex issib il-postijiet ta’ ħruġ.

source: They usually have special food, drink and entertainment offers, to keep guests in a good mood, and keep them at the premise.
reference: Ġeneralment huma jkollhom offerti ta' ikel, xorb u divertiment speċjali, biex iżommu lill-mistiednin f'burdata tajba, u jżommuhom fuq il-post.

source: Some venues offer alcoholic beverages on the house. However, drunkenness impairs judgement, and all good gamblers know the importance of staying sober.
reference: Xi postijiet joffru xorb alkoħoliku b’xejn. Madankollu, is-sakra tfixkel il-ġudizzju, u l-lagħba tajbin kollha jafu l-importanza li tibqa’ sobriju.

source: Anyone who's going to drive at high latitudes or over mountain passes should consider the possibility of snow, ice, or freezing temperatures.
reference: Kull min se jsuq f'latitudnijiet għoljin jew jaqsam il-passaġġi tal-muntanji għandu jikkonsidra l-possibbiltà li jkun hemm il-borra, is-silġ, jew temperaturi tal-friża.

source: On icy and snowy roadways, friction is low and you cannot drive as if you were on bare asphalt.
reference: Fuq toroq bis-silġ u l-borra, ma jkunx hemm ħafna frizzjoni u ma tistax issuq bħallikieku kont fuq asfalt mikxuf.

source: During blizzards, enough snow to get you stuck can fall in very little time.
