reference: Many observed rhythms in physiology and behavior often crucially depend on the presence of endogenous cycles and their production through biological clocks.

source: Ritmi perjodiċi, li mhumiex sempliċement risposti għal indikazzjonijiet perjodiċi esterni, ġew dokumentati għall-biċċa l-kbira tal-kreaturi ħajjin, inklużi batterji, fungi, pjanti u annimali.
reference: Periodic rhythms, which are not simply responses to external periodic cues, have been documented for most living beings, including bacteria, fungi, plants, and animals.

source: L-arloġġi bijoloġiċi huma oxxillaturi li jsostnu lilhom infushom li se jkomplu perjodu ta’ ċikliżmu ta’ ġiri liberu anke mingħajr indikazzjonijiet esterni.
reference: Biological clocks are self sustaining oscillators which will continue a period of free-running cycling even in the absence of external cues.

source: L-esperiment ta’ Hershey u Chase kien wieħed mis-suġġerimenti ewlenin li d-DNA kien materjal ġenetiku.
reference: The Hershey and Chase experiment was one of the leading suggestions that DNA was a genetic material.

source: Hershey u Chase użaw fagi, jew vajrusis, biex idaħħlu d-DNA tagħhom stess ġewwa bacterium.
reference: Hershey and Chase used phages, or viruses, to implant their own DNA into a bacterium.

source: Huma għamlu żewġ esperimenti billi mmarkaw jew id-DNA fil-batterjofagu b'fosfru radjuattiv jew il-proteina tal-batterjofagu b'kubrit radjuattiv.
reference: They did two experiments marking either the DNA in the phage with a radioactive phosphorus or the protein of the phage with radioactive sulfur.

source: Il-mutazzjonijiet jista’ jkollhom varjetà ta’ effetti differenti skont it-tip ta’ mutazzjoni, is-sinifikat tal-biċċa ta’ materjal ġenetiku affettwat u jekk iċ-ċelloli affettwati humiex ċelloli tal-linja tal-mikrobi.
reference: Mutations can have a variety of different effects depending on the type of mutation, the significance of the piece of genetic material affected and whether the cells affected are germ-line cells.

source: Mutazzjonijiet biss fiċ-ċelloli tal-linja tal-mikrobi jistgħu jiġu mgħoddija lit-tfal, filwaqt li mutazzjonijiet x’imkien ieħor jistgħu jikkawżaw mewt taċ-ċelloli jew kanċer.
reference: Only mutations in germ-line cells can be passed on to children, while mutations elsewhere can cause cell-death or cancer.

source: It-turiżmu bbażat fuq in-natura jattira nies interessati li jżuru żoni aturali sabiex igawdu x-xenarju, inklużi l-pjanti u l-annimali selvaġġi.
reference: Nature-based tourism attracts people interested in visiting natural areas for the purpose of enjoying the scenery, including plant and animal wildlife.

source: Eżempji ta’ attivitajiet fuq il-post jinkludu l-kaċċa, is-sajd, il-fotografija, l-osservazzjoni tal-għasafar, u żjarat fil-parks u l-istudju ta’ informazzjoni dwar l-ekosistema.
reference: Examples of on-site activities include hunting, fishing, photography, bird watching, and visiting parks and studying information about the ecosystem.

source: Eżempju ta' dan huwa li żżur, tieħu r-ritratti, u titgħallem dwar l-orangutani f'Borneo.
reference: An example is visiting, photographing, and learning about organgatuangs in Borneo.

source: Kull filgħodu, in-nies jitilqu mill-bliet iż-żgħar tal-pajjiż bil-karozzi biex imorru x-xogħol u jgħaddu minn ħdejhom persuni oħrajn li l-post tax-xogħol tagħhom huwa l-post li għadhom kemm telqu minnu.
reference: Every morning, people leave small country towns in cars to go their workplace and are passed by others whose work destination is the place they have just left.

source: F’dan ix-shuttle dinamiku tat-trasport kulħadd, b’xi mod, huwa marbut ma', u jappoġġja, sistema ta’ trasport ibbażata fuq karozzi privati.
reference: In this dynamic transport shuttle everyone is somehow connected with, and supporting, a transport system based on private cars.

source: Ix-xjenza issa tindika li din l-ekonomija massiva tal-karbonju ċaqilqet il-bijosfera minn wieħed mill-istati stabbli tagħha li appoġġja l-evoluzzjoni tal-bniedem għal dawn l-aħħar żewġ miljuni ta’ snin.
reference: Science now indicates that this massive carbon economy has dislodged the biosphere from one of its stable states that has supported human evolution for the past two million years.

source: Kulħadd jieħu sehem fis-soċjetà u juża s-sistemi tat-trasport. Kważi kulħadd igerger dwar is-sistemi tat-trasport.
reference: Everyone participates in society and uses transportation systems. Almost everyone complains about transportation systems.

source: Fil-pajjiżi żviluppati, rari tisma’ livelli ta' tgergir simili dwar il-kwalità tal-ilma jew pontijiet li jaqgħu.
reference: In developed countries you seldom hear similar levels of complaints about water quality or bridges falling down.

source: Għaliex is-sistemi tat-trasport joħolqu ilmenti bħal dawn, għaliex ifallu ta' kuljum? L-inġiniera tat-trasport huma sempliċiment inkompetenti? Jew qed jiġri xi ħaġa aktar fundamentali?
reference: Why do transportation systems engender such complaints, why do they fail on a daily basis? Are transportation engineers just incompetent? Or is something more fundamental going on?

source: Il-Fluss tat-Traffiku huwa l-istudju tal-moviment ta' sewwieqa u vetturi individwali bejn żewġ postijiet u l-interazzjonijiet li jagħmlu ma' xulxin.
reference: Traffic Flow is the study of the movement of individual drivers and vehicles between two points and the interactions they make with one another.

source: Sfortunatament, l-istudju tal-fluss tat-traffiku huwa diffiċli għax l-imġiba tas-sewwieqa ma tistax tiġi mbassra b'ċertezza ta' mija fil-mija.
reference: Unfortunately, studying traffic flow is difficult because driver behavior cannot be predicted with one-hundred percent certainty.

source: Fortunatament, is-sewwieqa għandhom tendenza li jġibu ruħhom f’medda raġonevolment konsistenti; għalhekk, il-flussi tat-traffiku għandhom tendenza li jkollhom konsistenza raġonevoli u jistgħu jiġu rappreżentati bejn wieħed u ieħor matematikament.
reference: Fortunately, drivers tend to behave within a reasonably consistent range; thus, traffic streams tend to have some reasonable consistency and can be roughly represented mathematically.

source: Biex il-fluss tat-traffiku jiġi rrappreżentat aħjar, ġew stabbiliti relazzjonijiet bejn it-tliet karatteristiċi ewlenin: (1) il-fluss, (2) id-densità, u (3) l-veloċità.
reference: To better represent traffic flow, relationships have been established between the three main characteristics: (1) flow, (2) density, and (3) velocity.

source: Dawn ir-relazzjonijiet jgħinu fl-ippjanar, id-disinn, u t-tħaddim tal-faċilitajiet tat-triq.
reference: These relationships help in planning, design, and operations of roadway facilities.

source: L-insetti kienu l-ewwel annimali li bdew itiru. Il-kapaċità tagħhom biex itiru għenithom jevitaw l-għedewwa b'mod iktar faċli u jsibu l-ikel u s-sieħba b’mod iktar effiċjenti.
reference: Insects were the first animals to take to the air. Their ability to fly helped them evade enemies more easily and find food and mates more efficiently.

source: Il-biċċa l-kbira tal-insetti għandhom il-vantaġġ li jkunu jistgħu jitwu l-ġwienaħ tagħhom lura f’posthom tul ġisimhom.
reference: Most insects have the advantage of being able to fold their wings back along the body.

source: Dan jagħtihom firxa aktar wiesgħa ta' postijiet żgħar biex jistaħbew mill-predaturi.
reference: This gives them a wider range of small places to hide from predators.

source: Illum, l-uniċi insetti li ma jistgħux jitwu lura l-ġwienaħ tagħhom huma l-mazzarell u l-mayflies.
reference: Today, the only insects that cannot fold back their wings are dragon flies and mayflies.

source: Eluf ta' snin ilu, bniedem imsejjaħ Aristarchus qal li s-Sistema Solari tiċċaqlaq madwar ix-Xemx.
reference: Thousands of years ago, a man called Aristarchus said that the Solar System moved around the Sun.

source: Xi nies ħasbu li kellu raġun imma ħafna nies emmnu l-oppost; li s-Sistema Solari mxiet madwar id-Dinja, inkluż ix-Xemx (u anke l-istilel l-oħra).
reference: Some people thought he was right but many people believed the opposite; that the Solar System moved around the Earth, including the Sun (and even the other stars).

source: Dan jidher sensibbli, għax id-Dinja ma tinħassx li qed tiċċaqlaq, mhux hekk?
reference: This seems sensible, because the Earth doesn't feel as if it's moving, does it?

source: Ix-Xmara tal-Amażonja hija t-tieni l-itwal u l-ikbar xmara fid-Dinja. Hija ġġorr iktar minn 8 darbiet l-ilma tat-tieni l-ikbar xmara.
reference: The Amazon River is the second longest and the biggest river on Earth. It carries more than 8 times as much water as the second biggest river.

source: L-Amażonja hija wkoll l-iktar xmara wiesgħa fid-Dinja, f’xi punti għandha wisa' ta' sitt mili.
reference: The Amazon is also the widest river on Earth, at times six miles wide.

source: B'kollox, mill-Amażonja jiġu 20 fil-mija tal-ilma kollu li jiġi mferra mix-xmajjar tal-pjaneta ġol-oċeani.
reference: A full 20 percent of the water that pours out of the planet's rivers into the oceans comes from the Amazon.

source: Ix-Xmara tal-Amażonja ewlenija hija 6,387 km (3,980 mil). Din tiġbor ilma minn eluf ta’ xmajjar iżgħar.
reference: The main Amazon River is 6,387 km (3,980 miles). It collects water from thousands of smaller rivers.

source: Għalkemm il-bini tal-piramidi bil-ġebel kompla sal-aħħar tas-Saltna l-Antika, il-piramidi ta' Giża baqgħu l-ikbar fid-daqs u l-aqwa fl-eċċellenza teknika tal-kostruzzjoni tagħhom.
reference: Although pyramid-building in stone continued until the end of the Old Kingdom, the pyramids of Giza were never surpassed in their size and the technical excellence of their construction.

source: L-Eġizzjani tal-antik tar-Renju l-Ġdid kienu mmeraviljati bil-monumenti tal-predeċessuri tagħhom li, dak iż-żmien, kellhom ħafna iktar minn elf sena.
reference: New Kingdom ancient Egyptians marvelled at their predecessors monuments, which were then well over a thousand year old.

source: Il-popolazzjoni tal-Belt tal-Vatikan hija ta' madwar 800. Hija l-iżgħar pajjiż indipendenti fid-dinja u l-pajjiż bl-inqas popolazzjoni.
reference: Vatican City's population is around 800. It is the smallest independent country in the world and the country with the lowest population.

source: Il-Belt tal-Vatikan tuża t-Taljan fil-leġiżlazzjoni u l-komunikazzjonijiet uffiċjali tagħha.
reference: Vatican City uses Italian in its legislation and official communications.

source: Il-lingwa Taljana hija wkoll il-lingwa ta’ kuljum użata minn ħafna minn dawk li jaħdmu fl-istat filwaqt li l-Latin spiss jintuża f’ċerimonji reliġjużi.
reference: Italian is also the everyday language used by most of those who work in the state while Latin is often used in religious ceremonies.

source: Iċ-ċittadini kollha tal-Belt tal-Vatikan huma Kattoliċi Rumani.
reference: All citizens of Vatican City are Roman Catholic.

source: In-nies ilhom jafu dwar elementi kimiċi bażiċi bħad-deheb, il-fidda u r-ram mill-antikità, peress li dawn kollha jistgħu jiġu skoperti fin-natura f’forma indiġena u huwa relattivament sempliċi li tħaffer għalihom fil-minjieri permezz ta’ għodod primittivi.
reference: People have known about basic chemical elements such as gold, silver, and copper from antiquity, as these can all be discovered in nature in native form and are relatively simple to mine with primitive tools.

source: Aristotli, filosfu, ħareġ bit-teorija li kull ħaġa hija magħmula minn taħlita ta’ wieħed jew aktar minn erba’ elementi. Dawn kienu art, ilma, arja u nar.
reference: Aristotle, a philosopher, theorised that everything is made up of a mixture of one or more of four elements. They were earth, water, air, and fire.

source: Dan kien aktar bħall-erba' stati tal-materja (fl-istess ordni): solidu, likwidu, gass, u plażma, għalkemm huwa tteorizza wkoll li huma jinbidlu f'sustanzi ġodda biex jiffurmaw dak li naraw aħna.
reference: This was more like the four states of matter (in the same order): solid, liquid, gas, and plasma, though he also theorised that they change into new substances to form what we see.

source: Il-liga hija bażikament taħlita ta' żewġ metalli jew aktar. Tinsiex li hemm ħafna elementi fit-tabella perjodika.
reference: Alloys are basically a mixture of two or more metals. Don't forget that there are many elements on the periodic table.

source: Elementi bħall-kalċju u l-potassju huma kkunsidrati metalli. Naturalment, hemm ukoll metalli bħall-fidda u d-deheb.
reference: Elements like calcium and potassium are considered metals. Of course, there are also metals like silver and gold.

source: Jista' jkollok ukoll liga li tinkludi ammonti żgħar ta' elementi mhux metalliċi bħall-karbonju.
reference: You can also have alloys that include small amounts of non-metallic elements like carbon.

source: Kull ħaġa fl-Univers hija magħmula minn materja. Kull materja hija magħmula minn partiċelli żgħar ħafna li jissejħu atomi.
reference: Everything in the Universe is made of matter. All matter is made of tiny particles called atoms.

source: L-atomi huma tant inkredibbilment miniskoli li triljuni minnhom joqogħdu fil-punt fi tmiem din is-sentenza.
reference: Atoms are so incredibly tiny that trillions of them could fit into the period at the end of this sentence.

source: Għalhekk, il-lapes kien ħabib tajjeb għal ħafna nies meta ħareġ.
reference: Thus, the pencil was a good friend to many people when it came out.

source: Sfortunatament, hekk kif ħarġu metodi iktar ġodda ta’ kitba, il-lapes ġiet relegat għal status iktar baxx u inqas użu.
reference: Sadly, as newer methods of writing have emerged, the pencil has been relegated to lesser status and uses.

source: In-nies issa jiktbu messaġġi fuq l-iskrins tal-kompjuter, u qatt ma jkollhom għalfejn jersqu lejn temprina.
reference: People now write messages on computer screens, never having to come close to a sharpener.

source: Min jaf x’se ssir it-tastiera meta tiġi vvintata xi ħaġa iktar ġdida.
reference: One can only wonder what the keyboard will become when something newer comes along.

source: Il-bomba tal-fissjoni taħdem fuq il-prinċipju li tittieħed l-enerġija biex nukleu jingħaqqad ma’ ħafna protoni u newtroni.
reference: The fission bomb works on the principle that it takes energy to put together a nucleus with many protons and neutrons.

source: B'mod simili bħal meta timbotta karrettun tqil fuq telgħa. Il-qsim tan-nukleu mill-ġdid imbagħad jirrilaxxa ftit minn dik l-enerġija.
reference: Sort of like rolling a heavy cart up a hill. Splitting the nucleus up again then releases some of that energy.

source: Xi atomi għandhom nuklei instabbli li jfisser li għandhom it-tendenza li jinqasmu bi ftit li xejn sforz.
reference: Some atoms have unstable nuclei which means that they tend to break apart with little or no nudging.

source: Wiċċ il-Qamar huwa magħmul minn blat u trab. Is-saff ta' barra tal-Qamar jissejjaħ il-qoxra.
reference: The surface of the Moon is made of rocks and dust. The outer layer of the Moon is called the crust.

source: Il-qoxra hija wiesgħa madwar 70 km fuq in-naħa l-iktar qrib u wiesgħa 100 km fuq in-naħa l-iktar ’il bogħod.
reference: The crust is about 70 km thick on the near side and 100 km thick on the far side.

source: Huwa irqaq taħt il-“maria” u eħxen taħt il-“highlands”.
reference: It is thinner under the maria and thicker under the highlands.

source: Jista' jkun hemm aktar ibħra tal-qamar fuq in-naħa viċina għax il-qoxra hija irqaq. Kienet aktar faċli għal-lava li titla' fil-wiċċ.
reference: There may be more maria on the near side because the crust is thinner. It was easier for lava to rise up to the surface.

source: It-teoriji tal-kontenut huma ċċentrati fuq is-sejba ta’ dak li jagħmel in-nies ferħanin jew jiġbidhom.
reference: Content theories are centered on finding what makes people tick or appeals to them.

source: Dawn it-teoriji jissuġġerixxu li n-nies għandhom ċerti bżonnijiet u/jew xewqat li ġew internalizzati meta jimmaturaw għall-istat ta' adulti.
reference: These theories suggest that people have certain needs and/or desires which have been internalized as they mature to adulthood.

source: Dawn it-teoriji janalizzaw x'inhu dak li lil ċerti nies iġegħlhom ikunu jridu l-affarijiet li jridu u liema affarijiet fl-ambjent tagħhom se jġegħluhom jagħmlu jew ma jagħmlux ċerti affarijiet.
reference: These theories look at what it is about certain people that make them want the things that they do and what things in their environment will make them do or not do certain things.

source: Żewġ teoriji tal-kontenut popolari huma t-Teorija tal-Ġerarkija tal-Ħtiġijiet ta' Maslow u t-Teorija taż-Żewġ Fatturi ta' Hertzberg.
reference: Two popular content theories are Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs Theory and Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory.

source: Ġeneralment, jistgħu jirriżultaw żewġ tipi ta’ attitudnijiet meta l-maniġers jibdew jimmaniġġjaw lil dawk li qabel kienu sħabhom. Tarf wieħed tal-ispettru huwa li wieħed jipprovaw jibqa’ jinħass bħala wieħed minnhom.
reference: Generally speaking, two behaviors can emerge as managers begin to lead their former peers. One end of the spectrum is trying to remain “one of the guys” (or gals).

source: Dan it-tip ta' maniġer isib diffikultà biex jieħu deċiżjonijiet mhux popolari, iwettaq azzjoni dixxiplinarja, iwettaq l-evalwazzjonijiet, jassenja r-responsabbiltajiet, u jara li n-nies ikunu responsabbli.
reference: This type of manager has difficulty making unpopular decisions, performing disciplinary action, performance evaluations, assigning responsibility, and holding people accountable.

source: Fin-naħa l-oħra tal-ispettru, wieħed jinbidel f’individwu li ma jintagħrafx li jħoss li għandu jbiddel kull għamel it-tim u jimmarkah bħala tiegħu.
reference: At the other end of the spectrum, one morphs into an unrecognizable individual that feels he or she must change everything the team has been doing and make it their own.

source: Wara kollox, il-kap huwa finalment responsabbli għas-suċċess jew għall-falliment tat-tim.
reference: After all, the leader is ultimately responsible for the success and failure of the team.

source: Din l-imġiba ħafna drabi toħloq firdiet bejn il-mexxejja u l-bqija tat-tim.
reference: This behavior oftentimes results in rifts between the leaders and the rest of the team.

source: Timijiet virtwali huma mistennija jissodisfaw l-istess standards ta' eċċellenza bħat-timijiet konvenzjonali, imma hemm xi differenzi sottili.
reference: Virtual teams are held to the same standards of excellence as conventional teams, but there are subtle differences.

source: Membri tat-tim virtwali ħafna drabi jiffunzjonaw bħala l-punt tal-kuntatt għall-grupp fiżiku immedjat tagħhom.
reference: Virtual team members often function as the point of contact for their immediate physical group.

source: Huma ħafna drabi għandhom aktar awtonomija minn membri ta' tim konvenzjonali peress li t-timijiet tagħhom jistgħu jiltaqgħu skont żoni tal-ħin li jvarjaw u dan jista' ma jinftiehemx mill-maniġment lokali tagħhom.
reference: They often have more autonomy than conventional team members as their teams may meet according to varying time zones which may not be understood by their local management.

source: Il-preżenza ta' "tim inviżibbli" veru (Larson u LaFasto, 1989, paġna 109) hija wkoll komponent uniku ta' tim virtwali.
reference: The presence of a true “invisible team” (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) is also a unique component of a virtual team.

source: It-“tim inviżibbli” huwa t-tim maniġerjali li kull wieħed mill-membri jirrapporta lilu. It-tim inviżibbli jistabbilixxi l-istandards għal kull membru.
reference: The “invisible team” is the management team to which each of the members report. The invisible team sets the standards for each member.

source: Għaliex organizzazzjoni tkun trid tgħaddi mill-proċess li tant jieħu ħin ta' twaqqif ta' organizzazzjoni li titgħallem? Wieħed mill-għanijiet għat-twettiq fil-prattika tal-kunċetti ta' organizzazzjoni li titgħallem huwa l-innovazzjoni.
reference: Why would an organization want to go through the time consuming process of establishing a learning organization? One goal for putting organizational learning concepts into practice is innovation.

source: Meta r-riżorsi disponibbli kollha jintużaw b'mod effettiv fid-dipartimenti funzjonali ta' organizzazzjoni, jistgħu joħorġu l-kreattività u l-inġenwità.
reference: When all available resources are effectively used across the functional departments of an organization, creativity and ingenuity can transpire.

source: Bħala riżultat, il-proċess ta’ ħidma flimkien ta’ organizzazzjoni biex jiġi megħlub ostaklu jista’ jwassal għal proċess innovattiv ġdid li jissodisfa l-ħtiġijiet tal-klijent.
reference: As a result, the process of an organization working together to overcome an obstacle can lead to a new innovative process to serve the customer's need.

source: Qabel organizzazzjoni tista’ tkun innovattiva, it-tmexxija trid toħloq kultura ta’ innovazzjoni kif ukoll għarfien kondiviż u tagħlim organizzattiv.
reference: Before an organization can be innovative, leadership must create a culture of innovation as well as shared knowledge and organizational learning.

source: Angel (2006), jispjega l-approċċ tas-Sekwenza Kontinwa bħala metodu li qed jintuża biex jgħin lill-organizzazzjonijiet jilħqu livell ogħla ta' prestazzjoni.
reference: Angel (2006), explains the Continuum approach as a method being used to help organizations reach a higher level of performance.

source: Dejta newrobijoloġika tagħti prova fiżika tal-approċċ teoretiku għall-investigazzjoni tal-konjizzjoni. Għalhekk, dan jagħmel il-qasam tar-riċerka inqas vasta u jagħmilha ferm iktar preċiża.
reference: Neurobiological data provide physical evidence for a theoretical approach to the investigation of cognition. Therefore it narrows the research area and makes it much more exact.

source: Il-korrelazzjoni bejn il-patoloġija tal-moħħ u l-imġiba tappoġġja lix-xjenzati fir-riċerka tagħhom.
reference: The correlation between brain pathology and behaviour supports scientists in their research.

source: Għal żmien twil kien magħruf li tipi differenti ta’ ħsara fil-moħħ, trawmi, leżjonijiet u tumuri jaffettwaw l-imġieba u jikkawżaw bidliet f’xi funzjonijiet mentali.
reference: It has been known for a long time that different types of brain damage, traumas, lesions, and tumours affect behaviour and cause changes in some mental functions.

source: L-iżvilupp ta' teknoloġiji ġodda jippermettilna li naraw u ninvestigaw l-istrutturi u l-proċessi tal-moħħ li qatt ma konna rajna qabel.
reference: The rise of new technologies allows us to see and investigate brain structures and processes never seen before.

source: Dan jipprovdilna ħafna informazzjoni u materjal għall-bini ta' mudelli ta' simulazzjoni li jgħinuna nifhmu l-proċessi f'moħħna.
reference: This provides us with a lot of information and material to build simulation models which help us to understand processes in our mind.

source: Għalkemm l-Intelliġenza Artifiċjali (AI) għandha konnottazzjoni qawwija ta' fantaxjenza, l-AI tifforma fergħa importanti ħafna tax-xjenza tal-kompjuter, u tittratta l-imġiba, it-tagħlim u l-adattament intelliġenti f'magna.
reference: Although AI has a strong connotation of science fiction, AI forms a very important branch of computer science, dealing with behavior, learning and intelligent adaptation in a machine.

source: Ir-riċerka dwar l-IA tinvolvi l-ħolqien ta' magni li jawtomatizzaw kompiti li jeħtieġu mġiba intelliġenti.
reference: Research in AI involves making machines to automate tasks that require intelligent behavior.

source: Eżempji jinkludu l-kontroll, l-ippjanar u l-iskedar, il-kapaċità li tingħata tweġiba lid-dijanjosi u l-mistoqsijiet tal-klijenti, kif ukoll ir-rikonoxximent tal-kalligrafija, il-vuċi u l-wiċċ.
reference: Examples include control, planning and scheduling, the ability to answer customer diagnoses and questions, as well as handwriting recognition, voice and face.

source: Affarijiet bħal dawn saru dixxiplini separati, li jiffokaw fuq is-sejbien ta’ soluzzjonijiet għal problemi tal-ħajja reali.
reference: Such things have become separate disciplines, which focus on providing solutions to real life problems.

source: Is-sistema tal-IA issa spiss tintuża fl-oqsma tal-ekonomija, il-mediċina, l-inġinerija u l-militar, kif inbniet f’diversi applikazzjonijiet ta’ softwer tal-kompjuters fid-djar u vidjowgejms.
reference: The AI ​​system is now often used in the fields of economics, medicine, engineering and the military, as has been built in several home computer and video game software applications.

source: Il-vjaġġi fuq il-post huma parti kbira minn kull klassi. Spiss, għalliema tħobb tieħu lill-istudenti tagħha f’postijiet li ma hemmx possibilità li tmur bil-karozza tal-linja fihom.
reference: Field trips are a large part of any classroom. Quite often a teacher would love to take her students places to which a bus trip is not an option.

source: It-teknoloġija toffri s-soluzzjoni bi vjaġġi virtwali fuq il-post. L-istudenti jistgħu jħarsu lejn artefatti ta’ mużew, iżuru akkwarju, jew jammiraw arti sabiħa waqt li jkunu bilqiegħda mal-klassi tagħhom.
reference: Technology offers the solution with virtual field trips. Students can look at museum artifacts, visit an aquarium, or admire beautiful art while sitting with their class.

source: Il-qsim ma’ ħaddieħor ta’ vjaġġ fuq il-post virtwalment huwa wkoll mod tajjeb ħafna kif tirrifletti fuq vjaġġ u taqsam esperjenzi ma’ klassijiet futuri.
reference: Sharing a field trip virtually is also a great way to reflect a on a trip and share experiences with future classes.

source: Pereżempju, kull sena studenti mill-Iskola Bennet ġewwa North Carolina jiddisinjaw websajt dwar il-vjaġġ tagħhom lejn il-Kapital tal-Istat, kull sena l-websajt tiġi mibdula mill-ġdid, iżda l-verżjonijiet l-antiki jinżammu online biex iservu bħala scrapbook.
reference: For example, each year students from Bennet School in North Carolina design a website about their trip to the State Capital, each year the website gets remodeled, but old versions are kept online to serve as a scrapbook.

source: Il-blogs jistgħu jgħinu wkoll biex titjieb il-kitba tal-istudenti. Għalkemm l-istudenti ħafna drabi jibdew l-esperjenza tagħhom tal-blogs bi grammatika u ortografija mhux tajba, il-fatt li jkollhom udjenza ġeneralment iwassal biex din is-sitwazzjoni tinbidel.
reference: Blogs can also help improve student writing. While students often begin their blog experience with sloppy grammar and spelling, the presence of an audience generally changes that.

source: Peress li l-istudenti ħafna drabi huma l-iktar udjenza kritika, il-kittieb tal-blog jibda jaħdem biex itejjeb il-kitba ħalli ma jiġix ikkritikat.
reference: Since students are often the most critical audience, the blog writer begins to strive to improve writing to avoid criticism.

source: Ukoll il-blogging "iġiegħel lill-istudenti jsiru jafu aktar dwar id-dinja ta' madwarhom." Il-bżonn li jissodisfaw l-interess tal-udjenza jispira lill-istudenti biex ikunu aktar jilħqilhom u interessanti (Toto, 2004).
reference: Also blogging "forces students to become more savvy about the world around them." The need to feed the interest of the audience inspires students to be clever and interesting (Toto, 2004).

source: Il-blogging huwa għodda li tispira l-kollaborazzjoni, u tħeġġeġ lill-istudenti jestendu t-tagħlim tagħhom ferm lil hinn mill-ġurnata tradizzjonali tal-iskola.
reference: Blogging is a tool that inspires collaboration, and encourages students to extend learning well beyond the traditional school day.

source: L-użu xieraq tal-blogs "jista’ jagħti s-setgħa lill-istudenti biex isiru iktar analitiċi u kritiċi; billi jirrispondu b’mod attiv għal materjali tal-Internet, l-istudenti jkunu jistgħu jiddefinixxu l-pożizzjonijiet tagħhom fil-kuntest ta’ kitbiet ta’ persuni oħrajn u jispjegaw il-perspettivi tagħhom dwar kwistjonijiet partikolari (Oravec, 2002).
reference: Appropriate use of blogs "can empower students to become more analytical and critical; through actively responding to Internet materials, students can define their positions in the context of others' writings as well as outline their own perspectives on particular issues (Oravec, 2002).

source: Ottawa hija l-belt kapitali inkantevoli u bilingwi tal-Kanada u fiha firxa ta’ galleriji tal-arti u mużewijiet li juru l-passat u l-preżent tal-Kanada.
reference: Ottawa is Canada's charming, bilingual capital and features an array of art galleries and museums that showcase Canada's past and present.

source: Aktar lejn in-Nofsinhar hemm in-Niagara Falls u lejn it-Tramuntana hemm is-sbuħija naturali mhux sfruttata tal-Muskoka u lil hinn minnha.
reference: Farther south is Niagara Falls and the north is home to the untapped natural beauty of the Muskoka and beyond.

source: Dawn l-affarijiet kollha u aktar jenfasizzaw lil Ontario bħala l-eżempju perfett ta' dak li jitqies bħala Kanadiż mill-barranin.
reference: All these things and more highlight Ontario as what is considered quintessentially Canadian by outsiders.

source: Inħawi kbar iktar lejn it-Tramuntana għandhom popolazzjoni pjuttost skarsa u xi wħud huma nħawi fi stat naturali kważi diżabitati.
reference: Large areas further north are quite sparsely populated and some is nearly uninhabited wilderness.

source: Għal paragun tal-popolazzjoni li tissorprendi lil bosta nies: Hemm aktar Afrikani Amerikani jgħixu fl-Istati Uniti milli hemm ċittadini Kanadiżi.
reference: For a comparison of population that surprises many: There are more African Americans living in the US than there are Canadian citizens.

source: Il-Gżejjer tal-Afrika tal-Lvant jinsabu fl-Oċean Indjan barra mill-kosta tal-Lvant tal-Afrika.
reference: The East African Islands are in the Indian Ocean off the eastern coast of Africa.

source: Madagascar huwa bil-bosta l-ikbar wieħed, u kontinent għalih meta nitkellmu dwar annimali selvaġġi.
reference: Madagascar is by far the biggest, and a continent on its own when it comes to wildlife.

source: Ħafna mill-gżejjer iż-żgħar huma nazzjonijiet indipendenti, jew assoċjati ma’ Franza, u magħrufin bħala resorts ta’ lussu fuq bajjiet.
reference: Most of the smaller islands are independent nations, or associated with France, and known as luxury beach resorts.

source: L-Għarab ġabu wkoll l-Iżlam fl-artijiet, u dan inxtered bil-kbir fil-Komoros u l-Mayotte.
reference: The Arabs also brought Islam to the lands, and it took in a big way in the Comoros and Mayotte.

source: L-influwenza Ewropea u l-kolonjaliżmu bdew fis-seklu 15, hekk kif l-esploratur Portugiż Vasco da Gama sab il-Cape Route mill-Ewropa għall-Indja.
reference: European influence and colonialism began in the 15th century, as Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama found the Cape Route from Europe to India.

source: Fit-Tramuntana, ir-reġjun huwa mdawwar bis-Sahel, u fin-Nofsinhar u fil-Punent bl-Oċean Atlantiku.
reference: In the north the region is bounded by the Sahel, and in the south and west by the Atlantic Ocean.

source: Nisa: Huwa rrakkomandat li kwalunkwe mara li qed tivvjaġġa tgħid li hija miżżewġa, hu x'inhu l-istat attwali tagħha rigward iż-żwieġ.
reference: Women: It is recommended that any women travellers say that they are married, regardless of actual marital status.

source: Huwa utli li tilbes ċurkett ukoll (biss mhux wieħed li jidher li jiswa ħafna flus.
reference: It is helpful to also wear a ring (just not one that looks too expensive.

source: In-nisa għandhom jifhmu li d-differenzi kulturali jistgħu jirriżultaw f'dak li huma jistgħu jqisuh bħala fastidju u mhijiex ħaġa rari li jiġu segwiti, maħtufin mid-driegħ, eċċ.
reference: Women should realize that cultural differences may result in what they would consider harassment and it is not uncommon to be followed, grabbed by the arm, etc.

source: Żomm sod meta tirrifjuta lill-irġiel, u tibżax tinsisti fuq il-preferenza tiegħek (kemm jekk hemm differenzi kulturali jew le, ma jfissirx li trid taċċetta kollox bħala tajjeb!).
reference: Be firm in turning down men, and don't be afraid to stand your ground (cultural differences or not, it doesn't make it ok!).

source: Il-belt moderna ta’ Casablanca twaqqfet minn sajjieda Berberi fis-seklu 10 Q.E.K., u l-Feniċi, ir-Rumani, u l-Merenidi użawha bħala port strateġiku msejjaħ Anfa.
reference: The modern city of Casablanca was founded by Berber fishermen in the 10th century BCE, and was used by the Phoenicians, Romans, and the Merenids as a strategic port called Anfa.

source: Il-Portugiżi kissruha u reġgħu bnewha taħt l-isem ta' Casa Branca, imma mbagħad abbandunawha wara terremot fl-1755.
reference: The Portuguese destroyed it and rebuilt it under the name Casa Branca, only to abandon it after an earthquake in 1755.

source: Is-sultan Marokkin reġa’ bena l-belt bħala Daru l-Badya u ngħatat l-isem “Casablanca” minn negozjanti Spanjoli li stabbilixxew bażijiet ta’ kummerċ hemmhekk.
reference: The Moroccan sultan rebuilt the city as Daru l-Badya and it was given the name Casablanca by Spanish traders who established trading bases there.

source: Casablanca huwa wieħed mill-inqas postijiet interessanti biex tixtri fil-Marokk kollu.
reference: Casablanca is one of the least interesting places to shop in all of Morocco.

source: Madwar il-Medina l-antika, faċli ssib postijiet li jbigħu oġġetti tradizzjonali tal-Marokk, bħal tagines, oġġetti tal-fuħħar, oġġetti tal-ġilda, hookahs, u spettru sħiħ ta’ geegaws, iżda kollha huma turistiċi.
reference: Around the old Medina it's easy to find places selling traditional Moroccan goods, such as tagines, pottery, leather goods, hookahs, and a whole spectrum of geegaws, but it's all for the tourists.

source: Goma hija belt turistika tar-Repubblika Demokratika tal-Kongo fil-Lvant estrem qrib ir-Rwanda.
reference: Goma is a tourist city of the Democratic Republic of Congo in the extreme east near Rwanda.

source: Fl-2002, Goma nqerdet b'lava mill-vulkan Nyiragongo li difen il-maġġor parti tat-toroq tal-belt, b'mod partikolari ċ-ċentru tal-belt.
reference: In 2002 Goma was destroyed by lava from the Nyiragongo volcano which buried most of the town’s streets, particularly the town centre.

source: Waqt li Goma hija raġonevolment sikura, kwalunkwe żjara 'l barra minn Goma għandha tiġi rriċerkata biex ikun magħruf l-istat tal-ġlied li għadu jippersisti fil-provinċja ta' Kivu tat-Tramuntana.
reference: While Goma is reasonably safe, any visits outside of Goma should be researched to understand the state of the fighting that persists in the North Kivu province.

source: Il-belt hija wkoll il-bażi biex tixxabbat mal-vulkan Nyiragongo flimkien ma’ ftit mill-irħas trekking tal-Gorilla tal-Muntanji fl-Afrika.
reference: The city is also the base to climb the Nyiragongo volcano along with some of the cheapest Mountain Gorilla tracking in Africa.

source: Inti tista' tuża l-bodaboda (mutur taksi) biex iddur ġo Goma. Il-prezz (lokali) normali huwa ~500 Frank Kongoliż għal dawra qasira.
reference: You can use boda-boda (motorcycle taxi) to get around Goma. The normal (local) price is ~500 Congolese Francs for the short ride.

source: Flimkien man-nuqqas ta’ aċċessibbiltà relattiva tagħha, "Timbuktu" ntużat bħala metafora għal artijiet eżotiċi u mbiegħda.
reference: Combined with its relative inaccessibility, "Timbuktu" has come to be used as a metaphor for exotic, distant lands.

source: Illum, Timbuktu hija belt imfaqqra, għalkemm ir-reputazzjoni tagħha tagħmilha attrazzjoni turistika, u għandha ajruport.
reference: Today, Timbuktu is an impoverished town, although its reputation makes it a tourist attraction, and it has an airport.

source: Fl-1990, ġie miżjud mal-lista ta’ siti tal-wirt dinji fil-periklu, minħabba t-theddida ta’ ramel tad-deżert.
reference: In 1990, it was added to the list of world heritage sites in danger, due to the threat of desert sands.

source: Kienet waħda mill-waqfiet ewlenin waqt il-sensiela speċjali tal-PBS “Wonders of the African World” ta’ Henry Louis Gates.
reference: It was one of the major stops during Henry Louis Gates' PBS special Wonders of the African World.

source: Il-belt tikkuntrasta b’mod qawwi mal-bliet l-oħra tal-pajjiż, minħabba li għandha elementi iktar Għarbin milli Afrikani.
reference: The city is in stark contrast to the rest of the country's cities, because it has more of an Arabic flair than of an African.

source: Il-Park Nazzjonali Kruger (KNP) jinsab fil-Grigal tal-Afrika t’Isfel u jgħaddi tul il-fruntiera tal-Mozambique fil-Lvant, iż-Żimbabwe fit-Tramuntana, u l-fruntiera tan-Nofsinhar hija x-Xmara tal-Kukkudrilli (“Crocodile River”).
reference: The Kruger National Park (KNP) lies in the north-east of South Africa and runs along the border of Mozambique in the east, Zimbabwe in the north, and the southern border is the Crocodile River.

source: Il-park huwa mifrux fuq 19,500 km² u huwa maqsum f'14-il ekożona differenti, u kull waħda fiha annimali selvaġġi differenti.
reference: The park covers 19,500 km² and is divided in 14 different ecozones, each supporting different wildlife.

source: Huwa wieħed mill-attrazzjonijiet ewlenin tal-Afrika t’Isfel u huwa meqjus bħala x-xempju tal-Parks Nazzjonali tal-Afrika t’Isfel (“South African National Parks” - SANParks).
reference: It is one of the main attractions of South Africa and it is considered the flagship of South African National Parks (SANParks).

source: Bħal fil-każ tal-Parkijiet Nazzjonali kollha tal-Afrika t’Isfel, jiġu applikati miżati ta’ kuljum għall-konservazzjoni u d-dħul fil-park.
reference: As with all South African National Parks, there are daily conservation and entry fees for the park.

source: Jista’ jkun ta’ benefiċċju wkoll li wieħed jixtri Wild Card, li tipprovdi dħul jew għal selezzjonijiet ta’ parks fl-Afrika t’Isfel jew għall-Parks Nazzjonali kollha tal-Afrika t’Isfel.
reference: It may also be beneficial for one to buy a Wild Card, which provides entry to either selections of parks in South Africa or all of the South African National Parks.

source: Il-Gżira ta’ Ħong Kong (“Hong Kong Island”) tagħti isimha lit-territorju ta’ Ħong Kong u hija l-post li bosta turisti jqisuh bħala l-fokus ewlieni.
reference: Hong Kong Island gives the territory of Hong Kong its name and is the place that many tourists regard as the main focus.

source: L-iskjerament tal-bini li jifforma l-iskajlajn ta' Ħong Kong ġie mxebbaħ ma' bar ċart b'dawl ileqq minħabba kif jidher bil-preżenza tal-ilmijiet tal-Port ta' Victoria.
reference: The parade of buildings that make the Hong Kong skyline has been likened to a glittering bar chart that is made apparent by the presence of the waters of Victoria Harbour.

source: Biex ikollok l-aqwa veduti ta' Ħong Kong, itlaq mill-gżira u mur il-waterfront ta' Kowloon fin-naħa l-oħra.
reference: To get the best views of Hong Kong, leave the island and head for the Kowloon waterfront opposite.

source: Il-maġġor parti tal-iżvilupp urban tal-Gżira ta’ Hong Kong hija ppakkjata b’mod dens fuq art reklamata max-xatt tat-Tramuntana.
reference: The great majority of Hong Kong Island's urban development is densely packed on reclaimed land along the northern shore.

source: Dan huwa l-post li ħadu l-kolonizzaturi Brittaniċi bħala tagħhom u għalhekk jekk qed tfittex evidenza tal-passat kolonjali tat-territorju, dan huwa post tajjeb fejn tibda.
reference: This is the place the British colonisers took as their own and so if you are looking for evidence of the territory's colonial past, this is a good place to start.

source: Is-Sundarbans huma l-akbar foresta tal-mangrovja littorali fid-dinja, twila 80 km (50 mil) mill-kosta sal-intern tal-Bangladexx u l-Indja.
reference: The Sundarbans are the largest littoral mangrove belt in the world, stretching 80 km (50 mi) into the Bangladeshi and Indian hinterland from the coast.

source: Is-Sundarbans ġew iddikjarati bħala Sit ta' Wirt Dinji tal-UNESCO. Il-parti tal-foresta fit-territorju Indjan tissejjaħ il-Park Nazzjonali tas-Sundarbans.
reference: The Sundarbans has been declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The part of the forest within Indian territory is called Sundarbans National Park.

source: Il-foresti mhumiex biss mangrovji f’artijiet mistagħdra — huma jinkludu xi wħud mill-aħħar pożizzjonijiet li għad fadal tal-ġungli imponenti li xi darba kienu jkopru l-pjanura Ganġetika.
reference: The forests aren't just mangrove swamps though — they include some of the last remaining stands of the mighty jungles which once covered the Gangetic plain.

source: Is-Sundarbans ikopru żona ta' 3,850 km², li madwar terz minnha hija miksija bl-ilma/żoni mistagħdra.
reference: The Sundarbans cover an area of 3,850 km², of which about one-third is covered in water/marsh areas.

source: Sa mill-1966 is-Sundarbans kienu santwarju tal-organiżmi selvaġġi, u huwa stmat li bħalissa hemm 400 tigra tal-Bengal Irjali u madwar 30,000 ċriev bit-tikek fiż-żona.
reference: Since 1966 the Sundarbans have been a wildlife sanctuary, and it is estimated that there are now 400 Royal Bengal tigers and about 30,000 spotted deer in the area.

source: Il-karozzi tal-linja jitilqu mill-istazzjon tal-karozzi tal-linja bejn id-distretti (tul ix-xmara) matul il-jum, għalkemm ħafna minnhom, speċjalment dawk li sejrin lejn il-Lvant u Jakar/Bumthang jitilqu bejn is-6:30 u s-7:30 ta’ filgħodu.
reference: Buses depart the inter-district bus station (across the river) throughout the day, though most, especially those heading to the east and Jakar/Bumthang leave between 06:30 and 07:30.

source: Peress li l-karozzi tal-linja bejn id-distretti ħafna drabi jkunu mimlijin, huwa rakkomandat li l-biljett tixtrih ftit ġranet minn qabel.
reference: As the inter-district buses are often full, it is advisable to purchase a ticket a few days in advance.

source: Bosta distretti fihom servizzi ta' Coaster Buses Ġappuniżi żgħar, li huma komdi u sodi.
reference: Most districts are served by small Japanese Coaster Buses, which are comfortable and sturdy.

source: Taksis maqsumin ma’ ħaddieħor huma mezz veloċi u komdu kif tivvjaġġa lejn postijiet fil-qrib, bħal Paro (Nu 150) u Punakha (Nu 200).
reference: Shared taxis are a quick and comfortable means to travel to nearby places, such as Paro (Nu 150) and Punakha (Nu 200).

source: Il-Pont tax-Xmara Oyapock huwa pont miżmum bil-kejbils. Huwa jaqsam ix-Xmara Oyapock biex jgħaqqad il-bliet ta' Oiapoque fil-Brażil u Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock fil-Guyana Franċiża.
reference: The Oyapock River Bridge is a cable-stayed bridge. It spans the Oyapock River to link the cities of Oiapoque in Brazil and Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock in French Guiana.

source: Iż-żewġ torrijiet huma għoljin 83 metru, huwa twil 378 metru u għandu żewġ karreġġati wisgħin 3.50 m.
reference: The two towers rise to a height of 83 meters, it's 378 meters long and it has two lanes of 3.50 m wide.

source: Il-vojt vertikali taħt il-pont huwa ta' 15-il metru. Il-kostruzzjoni tlestiet f'Awwissu tal-2011, ma fetaħx għat-traffiku qabel Marzu tal-2017.
reference: The vertical clearance under the bridge is 15 meters. Construction was completed in August 2011, it didn't open to traffic until March 2017.

source: Il-pont huwa skedat li jkun kompletament operattiv f’Settembru tal-2017, meta huma mistennija li jkunu lesti l-punti ta’ kontroll doganali Brażiljani.
reference: The bridge is scheduled to be fully operational in September 2017, when the Brazilian customs checkpoints are expected to be finished.

source: Il-Guaraní kienu l-iktar grupp indiġenu sinifikanti li jgħix f’dak li llum huwa l-Paragwaj tal-Lvant, li jgħixu bħala kaċċaturi semi-nomadiċi li pprattikaw ukoll l-agrikoltura ta’ sussistenza.
reference: The Guaraní were the most significant indigenous group inhabiting what is now Eastern Paraguay, living as semi-nomadic hunters who also practised subsistence agriculture.

source: Ir-reġjun Chaco kien fih gruppi oħra ta’ tribujiet indiġeni bħall-Guaycurú u Payaguá, li baqgħu ħajjin bil-kaċċa, il-ħsad u s-sajd.
reference: The Chaco region was home to other groups of indigenous tribes such as the Guaycurú and Payaguá, who survived by hunting, gathering and fishing.

source: Fis-seklu 16, il-Paragwaj, li qabel kienet tissejjaħ "Il-Provinzja Ġganta tal-Indji", twieldet bħala riżultat tal-laqgħa tal-konkwistaturi Spanjoli mal-gruppi indiġeni nattivi.
reference: In the 16th century Paraguay, formerly called "The Giant Province of the Indies", was born as a result of the encounter of Spanish conquerors with the native indigenous groups.

source: L-Ispanjoli bdew il-perjodu tal-kolonizzazzjoni li dam tliet sekli.
reference: The Spaniards started the colonization period which lasted for three centuries.

source: Sa mill-fondazzjoni ta’ Asunción fl-1537, il-Paragwaj irnexxielu jżomm ħafna mill-karattru u l-identità indiġena tiegħu.
reference: Since the foundation of Asunción in 1537, Paraguay has managed to keep a lot of its indigenous character and identity.

source: L-Arġentina hija magħrufa bħala li għandha wieħed mill-aqwa timijiet u plejers tal-polo fid-dinja.
reference: Argentina is well known for having one of the best polo teams and players in the world.

source: L-ikbar turnament tas-sena jsir f’Diċembru fil-kampijiet tal-polo ġewwa Las Cañitas.
reference: The largest tournament of the year takes place in December at the polo fields in Las Cañitas.

source: Turnamenti u logħbiet iżgħar jistgħu jidhru hawn ukoll fi żminijiet oħra tas-sena.
reference: Smaller tournaments and matches can also be seen here at other times of the year.

source: Għal aħbarijiet dwar tournaments u fejn tixtri biljetti għal-logħbiet tal-polo, iċċekkja l-Asociacion Argentina de Polo.
reference: For news on tournaments and where to buy tickets for polo matches, check Asociacion Argentina de Polo.

source: Il-munita uffiċjali tal-Falklands hija l-lira Falklandiża (FKP) li l-valur tagħha huwa ekwivalenti għal dak tal-lira Brittanika (GBP).
reference: The official Falklands currency is the Falkland pound (FKP) whose value is set equivalent to that of one British pound (GBP).

source: Il-flus jistgħu jiġu skambjati fl-uniku bank fil-gżejjer li jinsab fi Stanley biswit FIC West store.
reference: Money can be exchanged at the only bank in the islands which is located in Stanley across from the FIC West store.

source: Il-liri Ingliżi ġeneralment se jiġu aċċettati kullimkien fil-gżejjer u fi Stanley il-karti ta’ kreditu u d-dollari Amerikani huma wkoll aċċettati spiss.
reference: British pounds will generally be accepted anywhere in the islands and within Stanley credit cards and United States dollars are also often accepted.

source: Fuq il-gżejjer esterni, il-karti ta’ kreditu probabbli mhux se jiġu aċċettati, għalkemm jista' jkun li l-munita Ingliża u dik tal-Istati Uniti jiġu aċċettati; kellem lis-sidien bil-quddiem biex tara x’inhu metodu ta’ ħlas aċċettabbli.
reference: On the outlying islands credit cards will probably not be accepted, although British and United States currency may be taken; check with the owners in advance to determine what is an acceptable payment method.

source: Kważi impossibbli tibdel il-munita tal-Falklands barra mill-gżejjer, mela skambja l-flus qabel titlaq mill-gżejjer.
reference: It is nearly impossible to exchange Falklands currency outside of the islands, so exchange money prior to leaving the islands.

source: Peress li Montevideo tinsab fin-Nofsinhar tal-Ekwatur, hemm ikun is-sajf meta fl-Emisferu tat-Tramuntana tkun ix-xitwa u viċi versa.
reference: Since Montevideo is south of the Equator, it is summer there when it's winter in the Northern Hemisphere and vice versa.

source: Montevideo jinsab fis-subtropiċi; fix-xhur tas-sajf, temperaturi ’l fuq minn +30°C huma komuni.
reference: Montevideo is in the subtropics; in the summer months, temperatures above +30°C are common.

source: Ix-xitwa tista' tkun kiesħa b'mod qarrieq: it-temperaturi rari jinżlu taħt l-iffriżar, imma r-riħ u l-umdità jingħaqdu biex tinħass aktar kesħa minn dik li jindika t-termometru.
reference: The winter can be deceptively chilly: temperatures rarely go below freezing, but the wind and humidity combine to make it feel colder than what the thermometer says.

source: M'hemmx staġuni partikulari "bix-xita" u "nixfin": l-ammont tax-xita jibqa' bejn wieħed u ieħor l-istess tul is-sena kollha.
reference: There are no particular "rainy" and "dry" seasons: the amount of rain stays roughly the same throughout the year.

source: Għalkemm bosta annimali fil-park imdorrijin jaraw bnedmin, l-annimali selvaġġi xorta jibgħu selvaġġi u m'għandhomx jingħataw ikel jew jiġu ddisturbati.
reference: Though many of the animals in the park are used to seeing humans, the wildlife is nonetheless wild and should not be fed or disturbed.

source: Skont l-awtoritajiet tal-park, żomm mill-inqas 100 jarda/metru 'l bogħod mill-orsijiet u l-lupi u 25 jarda/metru 'l bogħod mill-annimali selvaġġi l-oħra kollha!
reference: According to park authorities, stay at least 100 yards/meters away from bears and wolves and 25 yards/meters from all other wild animals!

source: Indipendentement minn kemm jistgħu jidhru doċli, il-biżonti, l-elk, il-moose (ċriev kbar tal-Amerika tat-Tramuntana), l-orsijiet, u kważi l-annimali kollha li huma kbar jistgħu jattakkaw.
reference: No matter how docile they may look, bison, elk, moose, bears, and nearly all large animals can attack.

source: Kull sena, għexieren ta’ viżitaturi jkunu midruba minħabba li ma jżommux id-distanza xierqa. Dawn l-annimali huma kbar, slavaġ, u potenzjalment perikolużi, għalhekk tihom l-ispazju tagħhom.
reference: Each year, dozens of visitors are injured because they didn't keep a proper distance. These animals are large, wild, and potentially dangerous, so give them their space.

source: Madankollu, għandek tkun taf li l-irwejjaħ jiġbdu lill-orsijiet u annimali selvaġġi oħra, mela evita li ġġorr jew issajjar ikel b’irwejjaħ qawwijin u żomm il-kamp nadif.
reference: In addition, be aware that odors attract bears and other wildlife, so avoid carrying or cooking odorous foods and keep a clean camp.

source: Apia hija l-kapitali tas-Samoa. Din il-belt qiegħda fuq il-gżira ta' Upolu u għandha popolazzjoni ta' ftit inqas minn 40,000.
reference: Apia is the capital of Samoa. The town is on the island of Upolu and has a population of just under 40,000.

source: Apia twaqqfet f'nofs is-seklu dsatax u ilha l-kapitali uffiċjali tas-Samoa mill-1959.
reference: Apia was founded in the 1850s and has been the official capital of Samoa since 1959.

source: Il-port kien il-post fejn sar il-kunflitt infami navali fl-1889 fejn seba’ vapuri mill-Ġermanja, l-Istati Uniti u l-Gran Brittanja rrifjutaw li jħallu l-port.
reference: The harbor was the site of an infamous naval standoff in 1889 when seven ships from Germany, the US, and Britain refused to leave the harbor.

source: Il-vapuri kollha ġew mgħarrqa, minbarra krużer Brittaniku wieħed. Kważi 200 Amerikan u Ġermaniż tilfu ħajjithom.
reference: All the ships were sunk, except for one British cruiser. Nearly 200 American and German lives were lost.

source: Matul il-ġlieda għall-indipendenza organizzata mill-moviment Mau, laqgħa paċifika fil-belt irriżultat fil-qtil tal-kap ewlieni Tupua Tamasese Lealofi III.
reference: During the struggle for independence organised by the Mau movement, a peaceful gathering in the town resulted in the killing of the paramount chief Tupua Tamasese Lealofi III.

source: Hemm ħafna xtut bir-ramel, minħabba li Auckland qiegħda bejn żewġ portijiet. Dawk l-aktar magħrufa jinsabu fi tliet żoni.
reference: There are many beaches, due to Auckland's straddling of two harbours. The most popular ones are in three areas.

source: Il-bajjiet tan-North Shore (fid-distrett ta’ North Harbour) jinsabu f-Oċean Paċifiku u jibdew minn Long Bay fit-Tramuntana sa Devonport fin-Nofsinhar.
reference: North Shore beaches (in North Harbour district) are on the Pacific Ocean and stretch from Long Bay in the north to Devonport in the south.

source: Huma kważi kollha xtut bir-ramel b'għawm sikur, u l-maġġoranza għandhom id-dell ipprovdut mis-siġar tal-pohutukawa.
reference: They are almost all sandy beaches with safe swimming, and most have shade provided by pohutukawa trees.

source: Il-bajjiet ta’ Tamaki Drive jinsabu ġewwa l-Port ta’ Waitemata, fis-subborgi prestiġjużi ta’ Mission Bay u St Heliers fiċ-Ċentru ta’ Auckland.
reference: Tamaki Drive beaches are on the Waitemata Harbour, in the upmarket suburbs of Mission Bay and St Heliers in Central Auckland.

source: Dawn huma xtut bir-ramel għall-familja li kultant ikunu ffullati u jkollhom firxa tajba ta' ħwienet matul ix-xatt. L-għawm huwa sikur.
reference: These are sometimes-crowded family beaches with a good range of shops lining the shore. Swimming is safe.

source: Il-birra lokali ewlenija hija “Number One”, mhix birra kumplessa, iżda hija pjaċevoli u rinfreskanti. Il-birra lokali l-oħra msejħa "Manta".
reference: The main local beer is 'Number One', it is not a complex beer, but pleasant and refreshing. The other local beer is called "Manta".

source: Hemm ħafna nbejjed Franċiżi li tista' ssib, imma l-inbejjed ta' New Zealand u tal-Awstralja probabbilment jivvjaġġaw aħjar.
reference: There are many French wines to be had, but the New Zealand and Australian wines might travel better.

source: L-ilma tal-vit lokali tajjeb għax-xorb, imma faċilment issib ilma bbottiljat jekk għandek xi tħassib.
reference: The local tap water is perfectly safe to drink, but bottled water is easy to find if you are fearful.

source: Għall-Awstraljani, l-idea tal-kafè msejjaħ “flat white” mhix familjari. “Short black” huwa “espresso”, kapuċċino jiġi mimli b’ħafna krema (mhux ragħwa), u t-te huwa servut mingħajr ħalib.
reference: For Australians, the idea of 'flat white' coffee is foreign. A short black is 'espresso', cappuccino comes heaped high with cream (not froth), and tea is served without milk.

source: Iċ-ċikkulata sħuna hija skont l-istandards Belġjani. Il-meraq tal-frott jiswa ħafna flus iżda huwa eċċellenti.
reference: The hot chocolate is up to Belgian standards. Fruit juices are pricey but excellent.

source: Ħafna vjaġġi lejn is-sikka jsiru s-sena kollha, u l-korrimenti minħabba xi waħda minn dawn il-kawżi fuq is-sikka huma rari.
reference: Many trips to the reef are made all year around, and injuries due to any of these causes on the reef are rare.

source: Xorta, għandek tieħu parir mingħand l-awtoritajiet, timxi mas-sinjali kollha, u toqgħod attent/a ħafna għall-messaġġi tas-sigurtà.
reference: Still, take advice from authorities, obey all signs, and pay close attention to safety warnings.

source: Il-bram forma ta’ kaxxa (“box jellyfish”) jinstab ħdejn il-bajjiet u ħdejn l-estwarji tax-xmajjar minn Ottubru sa April fit-tramuntana tal-1770. Kultant wieħed jista’ jarahom ukoll barra minn dawn iż-żminijiet.
reference: Box jellyfish occur near beaches and near river estuaries from October to April north of 1770. They can occasionally be found outside these times.

source: Għalkemm il-klieb il-baħar jeżistu, rari jattakkaw lin-nies. Ħafna mill-klieb il-baħar jibżgħu min-nies u jgħumu ’l bogħod minnhom.
reference: Sharks do exist, however they rarely attack humans. Most sharks are scared of humans and would swim away.

source: Il-Kukkudrilli tal-Ilma Mielaħ ma jgħixux attivament fl-oċean, l-abitat primarju tagħhom huwa fl-estwarji tax-xmajjar fit-Tramuntana minn Rockhampton.
reference: Saltwater Crocodiles do not actively live in the ocean, their primary habitat is in river estuaries north from Rockhampton.

source: Jekk jibbukkja minn qabel, il-vjaġġatur ikollu moħħu mistrieħ li se jkollu x’imkien fejn jorqod malli jasal fid-destinazzjoni tiegħu.
reference: Booking in advance gives the traveller peace of mind that they will have somewhere to sleep once they arrive at their destination.

source: L-aġenti tal-ivvjaġġar ħafna drabi jagħmlu ftehimiet ma' lukandi speċifiċi, għalkemm inti tista' ssibha possibbli li tibbukkja forom oħra ta' akkomodazzjoni, bħal postijiet tal-ikkampjar, permezz ta' aġent tal-ivvjaġġar.
reference: Travel agents often have deals with specific hotels, although you may find it possible to book other forms of accommodation, like camping grounds, through a travel agent.

source: L-aġenti tal-ivvjaġġar ħafna drabi joffru pakketti li jinkludu l-brekfast, arranġamenti tat-trasport għal/minn l-ajruport u anki pakketti li jikkombinaw it-titjira u l-lukanda.
reference: Travel agents usually offer packages that include breakfast, transportation arrangements to/from the airport or even combined flight and hotel packages.

source: Huma jistgħu jżommu wkoll ir-reservation għalik jekk teħtieġ aktar żmien biex taħseb dwar l-offerta jew biex tikseb dokumenti oħra għad-destinazzjoni tiegħek (eż. viża).
reference: They can also hold the reservation for you if you need time to think about the offer or procure other documents for your destination (e.g. visa).

source: Kwalunkwe emenda jew talba pero għandha tgħaddi għand l-aġent tal-ivvjaġġar l-ewwel u mhux direttament għand il-lukanda.
reference: Any amendments or requests though should be coursed through the travel agent first and not directly with the hotel.

source: Għal xi festivals, il-maġġoranza l-kbira ta’ dawk li jmorru festivals tal-mużika jiddeċiedu li jikkampjaw fuq il-post, u ħafna minnhom jqisu li dan huwa parti essenzjali mill-esperjenza.
reference: For some festivals, the vast majority of the attendants to music festivals decide to camp on site, and most attendants consider it a vital part of the experience.

source: Jekk trid tara l-azzjoni mill-qrib trid tasal kmieni biex ikollok sit tal-kampeġġ qrib il-mużika.
reference: If you want to be close to the action you're going to have to get in early to get a camping site close to the music.

source: Ftakar li għalkemm il-mużika fuq il-palkijiet ewlenin tista’ tkun spiċċat, jista’ jkun hemm sezzjonijiet tal-festival li jibqgħu jdoqqu l-mużika sa tard bil-lejl.
reference: Remember that even though music on the main stages may have finished, there may be sections of the festival that will keep playing music until late into the night.

source: Xi festivals għandhom żoni ta’ kampeġġ speċjali għal familji bi tfal żgħar.
reference: Some festivals have special camping areas for families with young children.

source: Jekk tkun se taqsam il-Baltiku tat-Tramuntana fix-xitwa, ara sew il-post tal-kabina, minħabba li meta tgħaddu mis-silġ joħroġ ħoss orribbli għal dawk l-iktar affettwati.
reference: If crossing the Northern Baltic in winter, check the cabin location, as going through ice causes quite horrible noise for those most affected.

source: Il-kruċieri f’San Pietruburgu jinkludu ħin fil-belt. Il-passiġġieri tal-kruċieri huma eżentati mir-rekwiżiti tal-viża (iċċekkja t-termini).
reference: Saint Petersburg cruises include time in town. Cruise passengers are exempted from visa requirements (check the terms).

source: Il-każinos tipikament jagħmlu sforzi kbar biex jimmassimizzaw il-ħin u l-flus li jonfqu fih il-mistednin. Ġeneralment ma jkunx hemm twieqi u arloġġi u jista’ jkun diffiċli biex issib il-postijiet ta’ ħruġ.
reference: Casinos typically make many efforts to maximize time and money spent by guests. Windows and clocks are usually absent, and exits can be hard to find.

source: Ġeneralment huma jkollhom offerti ta' ikel, xorb u divertiment speċjali, biex iżommu lill-mistiednin f'burdata tajba, u jżommuhom fuq il-post.
reference: They usually have special food, drink and entertainment offers, to keep guests in a good mood, and keep them at the premise.

source: Xi postijiet joffru xorb alkoħoliku b’xejn. Madankollu, is-sakra tfixkel il-ġudizzju, u l-lagħba tajbin kollha jafu l-importanza li tibqa’ sobriju.
reference: Some venues offer alcoholic beverages on the house. However, drunkenness impairs judgement, and all good gamblers know the importance of staying sober.

source: Kull min se jsuq f'latitudnijiet għoljin jew jaqsam il-passaġġi tal-muntanji għandu jikkonsidra l-possibbiltà li jkun hemm il-borra, is-silġ, jew temperaturi tal-friża.
reference: Anyone who's going to drive at high latitudes or over mountain passes should consider the possibility of snow, ice, or freezing temperatures.

source: Fuq toroq bis-silġ u l-borra, ma jkunx hemm ħafna frizzjoni u ma tistax issuq bħallikieku kont fuq asfalt mikxuf.
reference: On icy and snowy roadways, friction is low and you cannot drive as if you were on bare asphalt.

source: Matul it-tempesti qawwija tas-silġ, f’temp qasir tista’ tinżel biżżejjed borra biex inti teħel.
reference: During blizzards, enough snow to get you stuck can fall in very little time.

source: Il-viżibilità tista' tkun restritta wkoll bil-borra nieżla jew ittir jew bil-kondensazzjoni jew is-silġ fuq it-twieqi tal-vetturi.
reference: Visibility may also be restricted by falling or blowing snow or by condensation or ice on vehicle windows.

source: Min-naħa l-oħra, il-kundizzjonijiet tas-silġ u tal-borra huma normali f’ħafna pajjiżi, u t-traffiku jibqa’ għaddej fil-biċċa l-kbira mingħajr interruzzjonijiet is-sena kollha.
reference: On the other hand, icy and snowy conditions are normal in many countries, and traffic goes on mostly uninterrupted all year round.

source: Is-safaris huma forsi l-ikbar attrazzjoni turistika fl-Afrika u l-punt ewlieni ta’ ħafna viżitaturi.
reference: Safaris are perhaps the greatest tourism draw in Africa and the highlight for many visitors.

source: It-terminu safari fl-użu popolari jirreferi għall-ivvjaġġar fuq l-art għall-osservazzjoni tal-annimali selvaġġi u meraviljużi Afrikani, partikularment fis-savana.
reference: The term safari in popular use refers to overland travel to view the stunning African wildlife, particularly on savanna.

source: Xi annimali, bħall-iljunfanti u l-ġiraffi, għandhom tendenza li jersqu lejn il-karozzi u qrib tagħmir standard li jippermetti veduti tajbin.
reference: Some animals, such as elephants and giraffes, tend to approach closely to cars and standard equipment will allow good viewing.

source: L-iljuni, iċ-ċitas u l-leopardi kultant huma mgerrxin u tarahom aħjar bil-binokli.
reference: Lions, cheetahs and leopards are sometimes shy and you will see them better with binoculars.

source: Safari bil-mixi (imsejjaħ ukoll "mixja fil-“bush” (“bush walk”)", "safari fuq hike (“hiking safari”)", jew “going "footing"”) jikkonsisti f’hike, jew għal ftit sigħat jew diversi jiem.
reference: A walking safari (also called a "bush walk", "hiking safari", or going "footing") consists of hiking, either for a few hours or several days.

source: Il-Paralimpiks se jsiru mill-24 ta' Awwissu sal-5 ta' Settembru tal-2021. Xi avvenimenti se jiġu organizzati ġewwa postijiet oħra fil-Ġappun.
reference: The Paralympics will take place from 24 August to 5 September 2021. Some events will be held in other locations throughout Japan.

source: Tokyo se tkun l-unika belt Asjatika li ospitat żewġ Olimpijadi tas-sajf, peress li diġà ospitat il-logħbiet fl-1964.
reference: Tokyo will be the only Asian city to have hosted two summer Olympics, having hosted the games in 1964.

source: Jekk ipprenotajt it-titjiriet u l-akkomodazzjoni tiegħek għall-2020 qabel ma tħabbar il-posponiment, jista’ jkollok sitwazzjoni kumplikata.
reference: If you booked your flights and accommodation for 2020 before the postponement was announced, you may have a tricky situation.

source: Il-politiki ta’ kanċellazzjoni jvarjaw, iżda mill-aħħar ta’ Marzu ħafna mill-politiki ta’ kanċellazzjoni bbażati fuq il-koronavirus ma jestendux sa Lulju 2020, meta kien ġew skedati l-Olimpjadi.
reference: Cancellation policies vary, but as of late March most coronavirus-based cancellation policies don't extend to July 2020, when the Olympics had been scheduled.

source: Huwa mistenni li l-biċċa l-kbira tal-biljetti tal-avvenimenti jiswew bejn ¥2,500 u ¥130,000, u biljetti tipiċi jiswew madwar ¥7,000.
reference: It's expected that most event tickets will cost between ¥2,500 and ¥130,000, with typical tickets costing around ¥7,000.

source: Il-mogħdija fuq ħwejjeġ niedja tista’ tgħinhom jinxfu. Bosta lukandi għandhom ħadida tal-mogħdija u bord biex tgħaddi fuqha disponibbli biex tużahom, anke jekk ma jkollokx waħda fil-kamra.
reference: Ironing damp clothes can help them dry. Many hotels have an iron and ironing board available for loan, even if one is not present in the room.

source: Jekk ma tkunx disponibbli ħadida tal-mogħdija, jew jekk ma tridx tilbes kalzetti mgħoddija, tista’ tipprova tuża hair dryer, jekk tkun disponibbli.
reference: If an iron isn't available, or if you don't fancy wearing ironed socks, then you can try using a hairdryer, if available.

source: Attent li ma tħallix id-drapp jiġi jaħraq wisq (li jista’ jikkawża li dan jixxrinkja, jew f’każijiet estremi, jinħaraq).
reference: Be careful not to allow fabric to become too hot (which can cause shrinkage, or in extreme cases, scorch).

source: Hemm diversi modi kif tippurifika l-ilma, ftit minnhom iktar effettivi kontra theddidiet speċifiċi.
reference: There are different ways of purifying water, some more effective against specific threats.

source: F’xi żoni, jekk tgħalli l-ilma għal minuta huwa biżżejjed, f’oħrajn hemm bżonn ta’ diversi minuti.
reference: In some areas boiling water for a minute is enough, in others several minutes are needed.

source: Il-filtri jvarjaw fl-effettività tagħhom, u jekk ikollok xi tħassib, għandek tikkunsidra li tixtri l-ilma tiegħek fi fliexken issiġillati minn kumpanija li jkollha isem tajjeb.
reference: Filters vary in effectiveness, and should you have a concern, then you should consider buying your water in a sealed bottle from a reputable company.

source: Il-vjaġġaturi jistgħu jiltaqgħu ma' annimali skifużi li huma mhumiex familjari magħhom fir-reġjuni fejn joqogħdu.
reference: Travellers may encounter animal pests that they are not familiar with in their home regions.

source: Il-pesti jistgħu jirrovinaw l-ikel, jikkawżaw irritazzjoni, jew f’każ agħar jikkawżaw reazzjonijiet allerġiċi, jagħtu velenu, jew jittrażmettu infezzjonijiet.
reference: Pests can spoil food, cause irritation, or in a worse case cause allergic reactions, spread venom, or transmit infections.

source: Il-mard infettiv stess, jew l-annimali perikolużi li jistgħu jweġġgħu jew joqtlu nies bil-forza, ġeneralment ma jikkwalifikawx bħala pesti.
reference: Infectious diseases themselves, or dangerous animals that can injure or kill people by force, do not usually qualify as pests.

source: Ix-xiri mingħajr dazju huwa l-opportunità li tixtri oġġetti eżentati mit-taxxi u s-sisa f’ċerti postijiet.
reference: Duty free shopping is the opportunity to buy goods exempted from taxes and excises at certain locations.

source: Il-vjaġġaturi li sejrin pajjiżi b'tassazzjoni qawwija jistgħu xi kultant jiffrankaw ammont konsiderevoli ta' flus, speċjalment fuq prodotti bħax-xorb alkoħoliku u t-tabakk.
reference: Travellers bound for countries with heavy taxation can sometimes save a considerable amount of money, especially on products such as alcoholic beverages and tobacco.

source: It-triq bejn Point Marion u Fairmont tippreżenta l-aktar kundizzjonijiet diffiċli tas-sewqan fuq l-Awtostrada Buffalo-Pittsburgh, għax tgħaddi ta' spiss minn wesgħat iżolati ta' backwoods.
reference: The stretch between Point Marion and Fairmont presents the most challenging driving conditions on the Buffalo-Pittsburgh Highway, passing frequently through isolated backwoods terrain.

source: Jekk m’intix imdorri ssuq fit-toroq tal-kampanja, oqgħod attent: hemm predominanza ta' għoljiet weqfin, korsiji dojoq u tidwir qawwi.
reference: If you're not used to driving on country roads, keep your wits about you: steep grades, narrow lanes, and sharp curves predominate.

source: Il-limiti tal-veloċità massimi huma notevolment iktar baxxi milli fis-sezzjonijiet preċedenti u sussegwenti — komunement 35-40 mph (56-64 km/siegħa) — u l-ubbidjenza stretta tagħhom hija saħansitra iktar importanti milli mod ieħor.
reference: Posted speed limits are noticeably lower than in previous and subsequent sections — commonly 35-40 mph (56-64 km/h) — and strict obedience to them is even more important than otherwise.

source: Madankollu, b'mod kurjuż, is-servizz tal-mowbajl huwa ħafna iktar qawwi hawn milli f’ħafna partijiet oħra tar-rotta, eż. il-Pennsylvania Wilds.
reference: Curiously, though, mobile phone service is much stronger here than along many other stretches of the route, e.g. the Pennsylvania Wilds.

source: Il-pasti Ġermaniżi huma pjuttost tajbin, u fil-Bavarja, huma rikki u varjati, simili għal dawk tal-ġar tan-Nofsinhar tagħhom, l-Awstrija.
reference: German pastries are quite good, and in Bavaria, are quite rich and varied, similar to those of their southern neighbor, Austria.

source: Il-pasti tal-frott huma komuni, u t-tuffieħ imsajjar jintuża fil-pasti s-sena kollha, u ċ-ċiras u l-għanbaqar jibdew fis-sajf.
reference: Fruit pastries are common, with apples cooked into pastries year round, and cherries and plums making their appearances during the summer.

source: Ħafna pasti Ġermaniżi fihom ukoll lewż, ġellewż, u ġewż ta' siġar oħrajn. Il-kejkijiet magħrufin spiss imorru sew ħafna ma’ kikkra kafè b'saħħtu.
reference: Many German baked goods also feature almonds, hazelnuts, and other tree nuts. Popular cakes often pair particularly well with a cup of strong coffee.

source: Jekk trid xi pasti żgħar iżda rikki, ipprova dawk li skont ir-reġjun jissejjħu Berliner, Pfannkuchen jew Krapfen.
reference: If you want some small though rich pastries, try what depending on region are called Berliner, Pfannkuchen or Krapfen.

source: Il-kari huwa dixx ibbażat fuq il-ħwawar, flimkien mal-laħam jew il-ħaxix.
reference: A curry is a dish based on herbs and spices, together with either meat or vegetables.

source: Il-kari jista' jkun "xott" jew "immerraq" skont l-ammont ta' likwidu.
reference: A curry can be either "dry" or "wet" depending on the amount of liquid.

source: Fir-reġjuni interni tat-Tramuntana tal-Indja u l-Pakistan, il-jogurt komunement jintuża fil-curries; fin-Nofsinhar tal-Indja u f’xi reġjuni kostali oħra tas-subkontinent, komunement jintuża l-ħalib tal-ġewż tal-Indi.
reference: In inland regions of Northern India and Pakistan, yogurt is commonly used in curries; in Southern India and some other coastal regions of the subcontinent, coconut milk is commonly used.

source: Minħabba li hemm 17,000 gżira, l-ikel Indoneżjan huwa terminu ġenerali li jkopri l-varjetà kbira ta' tisjir reġjonali li jinstab madwar il-pajjiż.
reference: With 17,000 islands to choose from, Indonesian food is an umbrella term covering a vast variety of regional cuisines found across the nation.

source: Iżda, jekk jintuża mingħajr kwalifikaturi ulterjuri, it-terminu għandu t-tendenza li jfisser l-ikel li joriġina mill-partijiet ċentrali u tal-Lvant tal-gżira ewlenija Java.
reference: But, if used without further qualifiers, the term tends to mean the food originally from the central and eastern parts of the main island Java.

source: It-tisjir Ġavaniż, li issa huwa disponibbli b’mod wiesa’ fl-arċipelagu, jinkludi firxa ta’ dixxijiet imħawwrin b’mod sempli, u l-aromatizzanti predominanti favoriti tal-Ġavaniżi huma l-karawett, iċ-ċilis, iz-zokkor (speċjalment iz-zokkor tal-ġewż tal-Indi Ġavaniż) u diversi ħwawar aromatiċi.
reference: Now widely available throughout the archipelago, Javanese cuisine features an array of simply seasoned dishes, the predominant flavorings the Javanese favor being peanuts, chillies, sugar (especially Javanese coconut sugar) and various aromatic spices.

source: L-istaffi huma sostenn għas-saqajn tar-rikkieb li jiddendlu fuq kull naħa tas-sarġ.
reference: Stirrups are supports for the rider's feet that hang down on either side of the saddle.

source: Huma jipprovdu stabbiltà akbar għar-rikkieb imma jista' jkollhom kwistjonijiet ta' sikurezza minħabba l-potenzjal li saqajn ir-rikkieb jeħlu fihom.
reference: They provide greater stability for the rider but can have safety concerns due to the potential for a rider's feet to get stuck in them.

source: Jekk rikkieb jaqa’ minn fuq żiemel iżda jkollu sieq maqbuda fl-istaffa, jista’ jiġi mkaxkar jekk iż-żiemel jaħrab. Biex jitnaqqas dan ir-riskju, jistgħu jittieħdu numru ta’ prekawzjonijiet ta’ sigurtà.
reference: If a rider is thrown from a horse but has a foot caught in the stirrup, they could be dragged if the horse runs away. To minimize this risk, a number of safety precautions can be taken.

source: L-ewwelnett, ħafna mir-rikkieba jilbsu boots tal-irkib b’għarqub u pett lixx u pjuttost dejjaq.
reference: First, most riders wear riding boots with a heel and a smooth, quite narrow, sole.

source: Sussegwentement, xi sruġ, b’mod partikolari sruġ Ingliżi, għandhom vireg tas-sigurtà li jippermettu li l-istrixxa tal-ġilda tal-istaffa tinqala’ minn mas-sarġ jekk tinġibed lura minn rikkieb li jaqa’.
reference: Next, some saddles, particularly English saddles, have safety bars that allow a stirrup leather to fall off the saddle if pulled backwards by a falling rider.

source: Cochamó Valley (Wied Cochamó) - Id-destinazzjoni ewlenija għat-tixbit taċ-Ċili, magħruf bħala l-Yosemite tal-Amerika t’Isfel, b’diversi ħitan kbar ta’ granit u blat.
reference: Cochamó Valley - Chile's premier climbing destination, known as the Yosemite of South America, with a variety of granite big walls and crags.

source: Il-aktar punti għoljin jinkludu veduti spettakolari mill-qċaċet. Xabbaturi mill-partijiet kollha tad-dinja kontinwament qed jistabbilixxu rotot ġodda fost il-potenzjal bla tmiem ta’ ħitan tiegħu.
reference: Summits include breath-taking views from peaks. Climbers from all parts of the world are continually establishing new routes amongst its endless potential of walls.

source: L-isports tas-silġ għan-niżla, li jinkludi l-iskijar u s-snowboarding, huma sports popolari li jinvolvi ż-żerżiq għan-niżla fuq art miksija bis-silġ fuq skis jew bord tas-silġ imwaħħlin ma’ saqajk.
reference: Downhill snowsports, which include skiing and snowboarding, are popular sports involving sliding down snow-covered terrain with skis or a snowboard attached to your feet.

source: L-iskijjar huwa attività ta’ vjaġġar ewlieni b’ħafna dilettanti, kultant magħrufin bħala "ski bums," li jippjanaw vaganzi sħaħ madwar l-iskijjar f’post partikolari.
reference: Skiing is a major travelling activity with many enthusiasts, occasionally known as "ski bums," planning entire vacations around skiing at a particular location.

source: L-idea tal-iskijar hija qadima ħafna — il-pitturi tal-grotta li juru skijaturi jmorru lura sal-5000 QK!
reference: The idea of skiing is very old — cave paintings depicting skiers date back as far as 5000 BC!

source: L-iskijar għan-niżla bħala sport imur lura mill-inqas sas-seklu 17, u fl-1861 infetaħ l-ewwel klabb tal-iskijar rikreazzjonali min-Norveġiżi fl-Awstralja.
reference: Downhill skiing as a sport goes back to at least the 17th century, and in 1861 the first recreational ski club was opened by Norwegians in Australia.

source: Backpacking bl-iskijjar: Din l-attività tissejjaħ ukoll “backcountry ski”, “ski touring” jew “ski hiking”.
reference: Backpacking by ski: This activity is also called backcountry ski, ski touring or ski hiking.

source: Huwa relatat ma’, iżda ġeneralment ma jinvolvix, touring ta’ skijar jew tixbit mal-muntanji ta’ stil alpin, fejn dan tal-aħħar isir fuq f’art wieqfa u jeħtieġ skis u boots ħafna iktar iebsin.
reference: It is related to but usually not involving alpine style ski touring or mountaineering, the latter ones done in steep terrain and requiring much stiffer skis and boots.

source: Ikkunsidra r-rotta tal-iskijjar bħala rotta simili tal-iħħajkjar.
reference: Think of the skiing route as of a similar hiking route.

source: F’kundizzjonijiet tajbin se tkun tista’ tkopri distanzi kemmxejn itwal milli kieku timxi – imma huwa biss rari ħafna li se jkollok il-veloċitajiet tal-iskijar “cross country” mingħajr backpack tqil f’binarji ppreparati.
reference: In good conditions you will be able to cover somewhat greater distances than walking – but only very seldom you will get the speeds of cross country skiing without a heavy backpack in groomed tracks.

source: L-Ewropa hija kontinent relattivament żgħir imma fiha ħafna pajjiżi indipendenti. Normalment, biex tivvjaġġa minn bosta pajjiżi jkollok tgħaddi minn applikazzjonijiet għal viża u kontroll tal-passaporti diversi drabi.
reference: Europe is a continent that is relatively small but with many independent countries. Under normal circumstances, travelling through multiple countries would mean having to go through visa applications and passport control multiple times.

source: Iż-żona Schengen, madankollu, taħdem xi ftit qisha pajjiż wieħed f'dan ir-rigward.
reference: The Schengen zone, however, works somewhat like one country in this respect.

source: Sakemm tibqa’ f’din iż-żona, ġeneralment tkun tista’ taqsam il-fruntieri mingħajr ma terġa’ tgħaddi mill-punti ta’ kontroll għall-verifika tal-passaport.
reference: As long as you stay in this zone, you can generally cross borders without going through passport control checkpoints again.

source: B’mod simili, billi jkollok viża ta’ Schengen, m’għandekx bżonn tapplika għal viżi għal kull wieħed mill-pajjiżi membri ta’ Schengen separatament, u b’hekk tiffranka ħin, flus u dokumentazzjoni.
reference: Similarly, by having a Schengen visa, you do not need to apply for visas to each of the Schengen member countries separately, hence saving time, money and paperwork.

source: M'hemm l-ebda definizzjoni universali għal liema oġġetti manifatturati huma antikitajiet. Xi aġenziji tat-taxxa jiddefinixxu l-oġġetti li għandhom aktar minn 100 sena bħala antikitajiet.
reference: There is no universal definition for which manufactured items are antiques. Some tax agencies define goods older than 100 years as antiques.

source: Id-definizzjoni għandha varjazzjonijiet ġeografiċi, fejn il-limitu tal-età jista’ jkun iqsar f’postijiet bħall-Amerika ta’ Fuq milli fl-Ewropa.
reference: The definition has geographic variations, where the age limit might be shorter in places such as North America than in Europe.

source: Prodotti tal-artiġjanat jistgħu jiġu definiti bħala antikitajiet, għalkemm huma iktar riċenti minn oġġetti simili prodotti fil-massa.
reference: Handicraft products might be defined as antiques, though they are younger than similar mass-produced goods.

source: It-trobbija tar-renni hija għajxien importanti fost is-Sámi u l-kultura madwar il-kummerċ hija importanti wkoll għal ħafna nies li għandhom professjonijiet oħra.
reference: Reindeer husbandry is an important livelihood among the Sámi and the culture surrounding the trade is important also for many with other professions.

source: Anke tradizzjonalment, minkejja dan, mhux is-Sámi kollha kienu involuti fit-trobbija fuq skala kbira tar-renni, iżda għexu mis-sajd, il-kaċċa u affarijiet simili, u kellhom ir-renni l-aktar bħala annimali biex jiġbdu piżijiet tqal.
reference: Even traditionally, though, not all Sámi have been involved in big scale reindeer husbandry, but lived from fishing, hunting and similar, having reindeer mostly as draft animals.

source: Illum ħafna Sámi jaħdmu fi snajja' moderni. It-turiżmu huwa dħul importanti f'Sápmi, iż-żona tas-Sámi.
reference: Today many Sámi work in modern trades. Tourism is an important income in Sápmi, the Sámi area.
